content.content:
1	# DIRECTION BRIEF — Kartar Rebuild (binding input to Sprint 01)
2	
3	> **Status:** BINDING for Sprint 1. This document pre-pins creative direction so the autonomous chain does not wander. Where this and improvisation conflict, this wins. Where this and measured engineering reality conflict, keep the creative intent, take the engineering prescription, and log the deviation in the sprint report.
4	> **Two prior overhauls were technically fine but creatively rejected** (one too close to the old saffron/indigo look, one a from-scratch "forge" concept that didn't land). The corrective: route-level competition in Sprint 1, a hard judge rubric, and an IA/showpiece/doctrine spine that is LOCKED so only *surface* (palette, type) is up for grabs.
5	
6	---
7	
8	## 0. NON-NEGOTIABLES (read first)
9	
10	- **Do NOT anchor on the old brand.** Forbidden as primary palette: saffron `#F2A93B`, indigo `#2D1B69`/`#4A2F9A`, teal `#00BFA5`, ember-orange `#FF5E0E`. The old logo/font/colors are not the brief. (A single accent may *coincidentally* be warm, but the page must not read as "the old site recolored.")
11	- **Doctrine: "The page is not scrolling — things are moving."** Every act must have a dominant travel direction that is NOT plain top-to-bottom. If an act is just "fade up in place," it is wrong. See §5.
12	- **Facts are a contract; words are free.** Every name, number, link, and integration in §2 must survive verbatim where it is an identifier (emails, IDs, URLs, person names, LinkedIn slugs, stat numbers). Marketing prose may be fully rewritten. The canonical machine-readable copy of the facts contract is `plans/00-facts/facts.json` (human-readable mirror: `plans/00-facts/FACTS.md`); the §2 table below is the design-side restatement and must not diverge from it.
13	- **No 3D artist, no paid assets.** Everything is procedural / typographic / shader-displaced / SVG / code-generated image sequences. See §4.
14	- **Mobile and reduced-motion never get a blank.** Every technique has a defined degraded form in §5.
15	
16	---
17	
18	## 1. THE CONCEPT SPACE
19	
20	**Brand meaning to express:** *Kartar = creator / builder / the one who makes.* Positioning = **"builders of autonomous AI for Indian industry."** The feeling we are selling is **competence that makes things real** — not mysticism, not generic SaaS gradients. The hero brand line **"All Is. Now"** survives as a fact (it can be re-set typographically, not reworded).
21	
22	Sprint 1 builds **three competing styleframe routes** (one scroll-complete vertical slice of the Home route each, same content, three different skins/motion), then the judge panel (§1.4) picks ONE. The three candidates are deliberately far apart so the winner is a real choice, not three shades of the same idea.
23	
24	### 1.1 Candidate A — "BLUEPRINT → BUILT" (Atelier / drafting-table)
25	
26	The site is a **drafting table where blueprints resolve into built reality.** Ghost wireframe linework, dimension marks, and annotations exist first; as you move, they *ink in*, fill with material, and snap into finished objects. This is the most on-brand for "builders" and the safest to execute well.
27	
28	- **Mood:** precise, confident, engineered. Quiet luxury of a well-made tool. Think technical drawing meets product photography.
29	- **Palette direction:** near-black **graphite ground** (`#0C0D10`-ish), **warm paper/bone** as the "built/inked" surface (`#EDE7DA`-ish), **one electric structural accent** that is the "live blueprint" line — pick **cyan-leaning blueprint blue** (`~#3B7CFF`) OR **acid lime** (`~#C8FF3D`) as the single saturated hue. Greys do the heavy lifting; the accent is ≤10% of any frame. (This blue is *blueprint*, semantically distinct from old indigo — and Sprint 1 may flip to lime to push further from the past.)
30	- **Type direction (self-hostable, Google Fonts / Fontshare only):**
31	  - Display: **Clash Display** (Fontshare) or **Fraunces** (Google) — choose the geometric one (Clash) to avoid serif-nostalgia from prior attempts.
32	  - Body: **Geist** (Google) or **Satoshi** (Fontshare).
33	  - Mono (dimension marks, labels, the "spec sheet" voice): **JetBrains Mono** or **IBM Plex Mono**.
34	- **Motion personality:** mechanical precision. Crisp ease (`cubic-bezier(.7,0,.2,1)`), short authoritative moves (0.5–0.7s), elements **snap** to grid lines (slight overshoot then lock). Linework draws on with `stroke-dashoffset`. Weighty, deliberate, never bouncy.
35	- **Borrows from:** obys.agency (kinetic technical type + grid), ten.375.studio (timeline/axis nav as the "ruler"), apple.com/airpods (assemble-from-parts).
36	
37	### 1.2 Candidate B — "GENESIS / RAW MATTER" (act-of-creation)
38	
39	The site is **formless matter that organizes into product as you move.** Particle fields, point clouds, and noise coalesce into legible objects (a logo, a knowledge graph, a product) then disperse as you leave. Most spectacular, highest WebGL risk — but the showpieces carry it.
40	
41	- **Mood:** cosmic-but-controlled, awe, emergence. Dark, deep, with light *born* from motion.
42	- **Palette direction:** **deep space charcoal/black** ground, **cool off-white** matter, and a **two-tone energy gradient** as the only color — pick a duotone NOT in the forbidden list: e.g. **violet→electric-blue** (`#7C5CFF → #2FD0FF`) or **magenta→amber-white**. Energy color only appears where motion is happening (the coalescing front), so the page is mostly monochrome with bursts.
43	- **Type direction:** Display **Boska** or **Hanken Grotesk** wide-weight (Fontshare/Google); Body **Hanken Grotesk** / **Geist**; Mono **Space Mono**. Big, airy, lots of negative space so the particle moments breathe.
44	- **Motion personality:** organic, eased, slightly slower (settles 1.0–1.4s), `expo.out` for coalescing, mass-and-drift physics for particles. Things *gather* rather than slide. Inertia and damping everywhere.
45	- **Borrows from:** lusion.co (progressive reveal pacing, depth), Codrops R3F image-reveal shader (matter→image), storytelling.noomoagency.com (chaptered emergence).
46	
47	### 1.3 Candidate C — "KINETIC INDEX" (editorial / type-as-machine)
48	
49	The site is a **kinetic editorial system** where giant typography is the primary moving object and 3D is used sparingly as punctuation. Horizontal pinned chapters, magnetic cursor, type that travels across the viewport on its own axis. Lowest WebGL risk, highest "Awwwards-typographic" payoff, most mobile-robust.
50	
51	- **Mood:** loud, confident, design-forward studio. Swiss-grid-broken-on-purpose. Editorial.
52	- **Palette direction:** **bone/paper light mode dominant** (`#F4F1EA`) with **near-black ink** — invert the usual dark-SaaS expectation to stand apart — plus **one hot accent** (oxblood `#C8472E`, or electric chartreuse). A light-dominant site is itself a differentiator from both prior dark attempts.
53	- **Type direction:** this route lives or dies on type. Display **Clash Display** + a contrasting **Editorial New / Gambetta** (Fontshare) for one expressive serif accent; Body **Satoshi**; Mono **JetBrains Mono**. Mix two display faces deliberately.
54	- **Motion personality:** snappy, kinetic, horizontal. Magnetic cursor, type that overshoots and settles, `xPercent` marquees coupled to scroll velocity, skew-on-velocity (the ONE place skew is allowed). Fast (0.4–0.6s).
55	- **Borrows from:** obys.agency + cuberto.com (magnetic cursor) + ten.375.studio (axis nav). Apple-style assemble showpiece is the single 3D moment.
56	
57	### 1.4 JUDGE-PANEL RUBRIC (how Sprint 1 picks the winner)
58	
59	Score each route 1–5 on each axis; weighted total picks the winner. **Tie → pick the more achievable route (C > A > B on risk).**
60	
61	| Criterion | Weight | What a 5 looks like |
62	|---|---|---|
63	| **Distinctiveness** | 25% | Does not read as the old site, nor as generic dark-SaaS. A stranger would not confuse it with a template. |
64	| **Fit to positioning** ("builders for Indian industry") | 25% | Communicates engineered competence + scale; not whimsical, not mystical. The 9 industry agents and CustomsIQ feel native to the aesthetic. |
65	| **Achievability by coding agents (no 3D artist)** | 25% | Showpieces are buildable from procedural/shader/type/SVG within draw budgets. No route depends on hand-authored 3D models, rigged characters, or bespoke illustration. |
66	| **Mobile degradability** | 15% | Each act has an obvious, non-blank mobile form. No act is "only good with WebGL." |
67	| **Execution confidence at sprint pace** | 10% | The styleframe slice already looks finished, not 60%. Few flaky dependencies. |
68	
69	**Build artifacts of Sprint 1:** each candidate route is authored as its own file under `plans/design/candidates/` — `route-A-blueprint.md`, `route-B-genesis.md`, `route-C-kinetic.md` (path form `plans/design/candidates/route-{A,B,C}-<name>.md`). Per-candidate styleframes (the rendered slices the judge reviews) go to `plans/design/styleframes/`.
70	
71	**Output of the judge step:** the verdict is written to `plans/design/JUDGE.md` — a one-paragraph verdict + the chosen route id (A/B/C). The chosen route's **final locked palette tokens and font choices** are written into `plans/design/TOKENS.md` (the FIRST_LIGHT_CONTRACTS §1 pattern — Tailwind token table + CSS vars + type scale). Everything downstream reads `plans/design/TOKENS.md`.
72	
73	---
74	
75	## 2. MULTI-PAGE IA (LOCKED — route names, content mapping, nothing dropped)
76	
77	> The identifiers in this section (route names, person names, LinkedIn slugs, stat numbers, integration IDs, links) are the design-side restatement of the facts contract. The canonical machine copy is `plans/00-facts/facts.json` (human mirror `plans/00-facts/FACTS.md`); on any conflict, `facts.json` wins and this table must be corrected to match.

GitHub Pages SPA fallback is mandatory: `public/404.html` redirect-trick (sessionStorage round-trip, the spa-github-pages pattern) + the SPA-restore snippet in `index.html`; **keep CNAME at repo root AND `public/CNAME`**; `BrowserRouter` (not Hash) since the 404 trick gives us clean URLs on Pages.

Now"**, dual CTA: Calendly + "See our work"); **CustomsIQ pipeline showpiece** (Read→Ground→Reason→Classify→Cite, the 9 knowledge-graph nodes); LogoBar **8 industries** (Logistics, Legal Tech, Healthcare, Finance, Agriculture, E-commerce, Manufacturing, Education); **3 Services** (Agentic AI Workflows, Vertical SaaS Products, AI Strategy & Scoping); CtaBanner; **3 Testimonials** (CTO Fintech; Operations Head Logistics; Founder Legal Tech); footer. |

The "builders" identity. | **WorkingProcess 4 steps** (Consultation/Research/Strategy → Build Complete → Ship & Measure → Feedback & Iterate); **Team 18 members** with LinkedIn URLs + initials-fallback avatars (Sachmeet Singh Bhatia, Priyank Verma, Astha Porwal, Shovan Mohapatra, Karuna Patil, Abhimanyu Kaundal, Anshul Bharti, Akansha Sarkar, Aditya Gupta, Rishi Mehta, Rakshit Baveja, Poonam Pandey, Shubh Bharadwaj, Shubhankar Anuragi, Rahul Tiwari, Sakshi Joshi, Nipun Katyal, Yugpratap Singh Pawar). |

87	
88	**Global (every route):** Navbar (links: Home, Work, Studio, Contact — **single source of truth, import from `src/data/nav.js`**; kills the old Navbar/Footer duplication), Footer (Sender.net newsletter form id `axkAXn`, account `08a8ce8a190275`; LinkedIn company link; **Calendly URL must come from one `src/data/links.js` constant** — it appears in 4 places today, dedupe to one). Preloader plays once on first load only (sessionStorage gate), NOT per route.
89	
90	**De-duplication mandate:** create `src/data/` (new — repo currently has zero data files): `nav.js`, `links.js` (Calendly, email, LinkedIn, Sender ids, EmailJS ids), `team.js`, `industries.js`, `caseStudies.js`, `services.js`, `process.js`, `testimonials.js`, `pipeline.js`. Sprint sections import from these. This is the FACTS CONTRACT made executable.
91	
92	**Page-transition concept (LOCKED mechanism, route-skin per winner):** a **chapter-wipe** transition. On route change, a full-bleed overlay (the route's signature material — blueprint paper for A, particle dissolve for B, ink-block for C) sweeps **horizontally** across the viewport, covers, swaps content at cover-peak, reveals on the way out, total ≤ 700ms. Implemented with framer-motion `AnimatePresence` on a route wrapper + a single GSAP timeline for the wipe; scroll resets to top *under cover* (never a visible jump). Reduced-motion = instant cross-fade ≤ 200ms, no wipe.

97

141	## 4. ASSET STRATEGY (no 3D artist, no paid assets)
142	
143	- **CustomsIQ graph (H2):** pure procedural — nodes are instanced low-poly spheres/rounded quads, edges are `Line2`/`LineSegments`, labels are drei `Html` (DOM, crisp text, no font-in-GL) or MSDF text via `troika-three-text` only if `Html` causes z-fighting. Node positions come straight from `src/data/pipeline.js` (the existing `{id,x,y,t,step}` table, re-spaced for 3D). **Zero external models.**
144	- **Skyline (W2):** one instanced geometry (box/rounded-box), per-instance transform + color. If image-reveal is used, the "after" images are **generated by code** (canvas-drawn stat cards / SVG-rasterized), not photography.
145	- **Hero object (H1):** procedural — wireframe polyhedron (Candidate A), GPU particle nucleus (B), or extruded type slab (C). All shader/geometry, no assets.
146	- **Image sequences:** only if code-generatable. Prefer live R3F scrub over pre-rendered frames. If a sequence is unavoidable, generate frames programmatically (canvas) at build time, ≤ 60 frames, WebP, ≤ 1280px long edge.
147	- **Typography-as-object:** giant type IS the art on Candidate C; SVG strokes are the art on Candidate A.
148	- **Fonts — self-hosting plan:** download chosen families from Fontshare/Google as **WOFF2 only**, place in `public/fonts/`, `@font-face` with `font-display: swap`, subset to Latin + the few glyphs used in mono labels. Budget: ≤ 4 font files, ≤ 220KB total. No Google Fonts CDN link (offline-safe, no layout-shift from network). **All motion that splits/measures text waits on `document.fonts.ready`** (scar: injected styles + late fonts cause re-measure jank).
149	- **Byte budgets (transfer, gzipped):**
150	  - Initial Home route (no GL yet): **JS ≤ 180KB**, CSS ≤ 30KB, fonts ≤ 220KB.
151	  - `three` + `@react-three/fiber` + `drei` chunk: **lazy, ≤ 320KB gzip**, fetched only on `full`/`mid` tier and only when a showpiece section nears viewport.
152	  - `@react-three/rapier` (**NEW dep**): only if a showpiece needs physics; gated behind the showpiece's own `lazy()` and `tier==="full"`. If H2/W2 don't need true physics (they don't — scrub-driven transforms suffice), **do not add rapier at all.** Default: no rapier.
153	  - Per-showpiece lazy chunk ≤ 120KB on top of the shared GL chunk.
154	  - Total page transfer on `full` tier ≤ 1.1MB; on `lite`/mobile ≤ 420KB (zero three bytes).
155	
156	---
157	
158	## 5. MOTION DOCTRINE (the anti-flaky laws — adapted from FIRST_LIGHT)
159	
160	### 5.1 The laws (LOCKED)
161	
162	1. **One easing language.** Define once in `src/lib/motion.js`: a single `PRIMARY` cubic-bezier for entrances/strikes and `SETTLE = "expo.out"` for cooling/settling. Register a GSAP `CustomEase` to match. No ad-hoc beziers in section files.
163	2. **Scrub over trigger.** Anything tied to scroll position uses a scrubbed ScrollTrigger. Trigger-once is reserved for "strike" entrances, which then **HOLD their end state forever** (the "forged-stays-forged" law: no re-trigger, no fade-out, no reverse on scroll-up except for the explicitly reversible showpieces H2/W1/W2).
164	3. **High-water latch.** Reveal progress only grows (latched), so fast scrolls never leave an act half-built. Scrub-latch triggers MUST have `onLeave`/`onLeaveBack` latch (scar #5).
165	4. **Velocity awareness.** Marquees and skews read a single shared `scrollBus.velocity`; one writer, many readers. Velocity decays to zero within ~1.5s of rest.
166	5. **No standalone rAF loops.** Lenis is raf-driven from `gsap.ticker` (current architecture). All per-frame work hangs off `gsap.ticker` or `useFrame`. **Never** a bare `requestAnimationFrame` loop.
167	6. **Zero per-frame allocations.** Module-level scratch objects, pre-allocated typed arrays in `useMemo`, mutate-never-replace for vectors/colors (`.set()`), `dt = Math.min(delta, 0.05)`.
168	7. **No per-frame `getBoundingClientRect`.** Measure on `ScrollTrigger.refresh`/resize only; cache rects.
169	8. **ScrollTrigger scopes the REAL window.** Lenis scrolls the real window (NO scrollerProxy today). **Do NOT move scroll into a transformed wrapper** — it silently breaks every pin (scar #2). Page-transition wipes are overlays, not scroll containers.
170	9. **Never `self.kill()` synchronously inside `onUpdate`** — corrupts trigger iteration and silently freezes later triggers. Defer: `gsap.delayedCall(0, () => trigger.kill())` (scar #1).
171	10. **Coordinated refresh.** Call `ScrollTrigger.refresh()` once after fonts ready + images decoded + preloader done; never per-section uncoordinated refreshes (scar #3).
172	11. **`position: relative`** on any section hosting an `absolute inset-0` overlay/veil (scar #4).
173	12. **Injected `<style>` outranks Tailwind** — prefer Tailwind utilities; if you must inject, scope it and know it wins the cascade (scar #6).
174	13. **StrictMode-idempotent.** Every imperative effect must be safe under double-invoke; use `gsap.context()` with revert cleanup (scar #7).
175	14. **Minimums.** Nothing animates under 0.3s. Minimum perceptible move = 40px or a full material/state change. No sub-pixel fidgets.
176	
177	### 5.2 Multi-directional doctrine (LOCKED)
178	
179	- Every act declares a **dominant travel vector** that is not plain vertical-with-scroll. Allowed: left, right, diagonal, depth (z), centripetal, counter-parallax. At least **3 acts per route** must move horizontally or in depth.
180	- Background and foreground must travel at different rates and ideally **opposite directions** (counter-parallax) so the eye never reads "a tall column scrolling up."
181	- Pinned acts (H2, W1, W2, S1) convert vertical scroll into lateral/assembly motion — this is the core "page is not scrolling, things are moving" mechanism.
182	
183	### 5.3 Reduced-motion / mobile contract (LOCKED — never blank)
184

# DIRECTION BRIEF — Kartar Rebuild (binding input to Sprint 01)

> **Status:** BINDING for Sprint 1. This document pre-pins creative direction so the autonomous chain does not wander. Where this and improvisation conflict, this wins. Where this and measured engineering reality conflict, keep the creative intent, take the engineering prescription, and log the deviation in the sprint report.
> **Two prior overhauls were technically fine but creatively rejected** (one too close to the old saffron/indigo look, one a from-scratch "forge" concept that didn't land). The corrective: route-level competition in Sprint 1, a hard judge rubric, and an IA/showpiece/doctrine spine that is LOCKED so only *surface* (palette, type) is up for grabs.

---

## 0. NON-NEGOTIABLES (read first)

- **Do NOT anchor on the old brand.** Forbidden as primary palette: saffron `#F2A93B`, indigo `#2D1B69`/`#4A2F9A`, teal `#00BFA5`, ember-orange `#FF5E0E`. The old logo/font/colors are not the brief. (A single accent may *coincidentally* be warm, but the page must not read as "the old site recolored.")
- **Doctrine: "The page is not scrolling — things are moving."** Every act must have a dominant travel direction that is NOT plain top-to-bottom. If an act is just "fade up in place," it is wrong. See §5.
- **Facts are a contract; words are free.** Every name, number, link, and integration in §2 must survive verbatim where it is an identifier (emails, IDs, URLs, person names, LinkedIn slugs, stat numbers). Marketing prose may be fully rewritten. The canonical machine-readable copy of the facts contract is `plans/00-facts/facts.json` (human-readable mirror: `plans/00-facts/FACTS.md`); the §2 table below is the design-side restatement and must not diverge from it.
- **No 3D artist, no paid assets.** Everything is procedural / typographic / shader-displaced / SVG / code-generated image sequences. See §4.
- **Mobile and reduced-motion never get a blank.** Every technique has a defined degraded form in §5.

---

## 1. THE CONCEPT SPACE

**Brand meaning to express:** *Kartar = creator / builder / the one who makes.* Positioning = **"builders of autonomous AI for Indian industry."** The feeling we are selling is **competence that makes things real** — not mysticism, not generic SaaS gradients. The hero brand line **"All Is. Now"** survives as a fact (it can be re-set typographically, not reworded).

Sprint 1 builds **three competing styleframe routes** (one scroll-complete vertical slice of the Home route each, same content, three different skins/motion), then the judge panel (§1.4) picks ONE. The three candidates are deliberately far apart so the winner is a real choice, not three shades of the same idea.

### 1.1 Candidate A — "BLUEPRINT → BUILT" (Atelier / drafting-table)

The site is a **drafting table where blueprints resolve into built reality.** Ghost wireframe linework, dimension marks, and annotations exist first; as you move, they *ink in*, fill with material, and snap into finished objects. This is the most on-brand for "builders" and the safest to execute well.

- **Mood:** precise, confident, engineered. Quiet luxury of a well-made tool. Think technical drawing meets product photography.
- **Palette direction:** near-black **graphite ground** (`#0C0D10`-ish), **warm paper/bone** as the "built/inked" surface (`#EDE7DA`-ish), **one electric structural accent** that is the "live blueprint" line — pick **cyan-leaning blueprint blue** (`~#3B7CFF`) OR **acid lime** (`~#C8FF3D`) as the single saturated hue. Greys do the heavy lifting; the accent is ≤10% of any frame. (This blue is *blueprint*, semantically distinct from old indigo — and Sprint 1 may flip to lime to push further from the past.)
- **Type direction (self-hostable, Google Fonts / Fontshare only):**
  - Display: **Clash Display** (Fontshare) or **Fraunces** (Google) — choose the geometric one (Clash) to avoid serif-nostalgia from prior attempts.
  - Body: **Geist** (Google) or **Satoshi** (Fontshare).
  - Mono (dimension marks, labels, the "spec sheet" voice): **JetBrains Mono** or **IBM Plex Mono**.
- **Motion personality:** mechanical precision. Crisp ease (`cubic-bezier(.7,0,.2,1)`), short authoritative moves (0.5–0.7s), elements **snap** to grid lines (slight overshoot then lock). Linework draws on with `stroke-dashoffset`. Weighty, deliberate, never bouncy.
- **Borrows from:** obys.agency (kinetic technical type + grid), ten.375.studio (timeline/axis nav as the "ruler"), apple.com/airpods (assemble-from-parts).

### 1.2 Candidate B — "GENESIS / RAW MATTER" (act-of-creation)

The site is **formless matter that organizes into product as you move.** Particle fields, point clouds, and noise coalesce into legible objects (a logo, a knowledge graph, a product) then disperse as you leave. Most spectacular, highest WebGL risk — but the showpieces carry it.

- **Mood:** cosmic-but-controlled, awe, emergence. Dark, deep, with light *born* from motion.
- **Palette direction:** **deep space charcoal/black** ground, **cool off-white** matter, and a **two-tone energy gradient** as the only color — pick a duotone NOT in the forbidden list: e.g. **violet→electric-blue** (`#7C5CFF → #2FD0FF`) or **magenta→amber-white**. Energy color only appears where motion is happening (the coalescing front), so the page is mostly monochrome with bursts.
- **Type direction:** Display **Boska** or **Hanken Grotesk** wide-weight (Fontshare/Google); Body **Hanken Grotesk** / **Geist**; Mono **Space Mono**. Big, airy, lots of negative space so the particle moments breathe.
- **Motion personality:** organic, eased, slightly slower (settles 1.0–1.4s), `expo.out` for coalescing, mass-and-drift physics for particles. Things *gather* rather than slide. Inertia and damping everywhere.
- **Borrows from:** lusion.co (progressive reveal pacing, depth), Codrops R3F image-reveal shader (matter→image), storytelling.noomoagency.com (chaptered emergence).

### 1.3 Candidate C — "KINETIC INDEX" (editorial / type-as-machine)

The site is a **kinetic editorial system** where giant typography is the primary moving object and 3D is used sparingly as punctuation. Horizontal pinned chapters, magnetic cursor, type that travels across the viewport on its own axis. Lowest WebGL risk, highest "Awwwards-typographic" payoff, most mobile-robust.

- **Mood:** loud, confident, design-forward studio. Swiss-grid-broken-on-purpose. Editorial.
- **Palette direction:** **bone/paper light mode dominant** (`#F4F1EA`) with **near-black ink** — invert the usual dark-SaaS expectation to stand apart — plus **one hot accent** (oxblood `#C8472E`, or electric chartreuse). A light-dominant site is itself a differentiator from both prior dark attempts.
- **Type direction:** this route lives or dies on type. Display **Clash Display** + a contrasting **Editorial New / Gambetta** (Fontshare) for one expressive serif accent; Body **Satoshi**; Mono **JetBrains Mono**. Mix two display faces deliberately.
- **Motion personality:** snappy, kinetic, horizontal. Magnetic cursor, type that overshoots and settles, `xPercent` marquees coupled to scroll velocity, skew-on-velocity (the ONE place skew is allowed). Fast (0.4–0.6s).
- **Borrows from:** obys.agency + cuberto.com (magnetic cursor) + ten.375.studio (axis nav). Apple-style assemble showpiece is the single 3D moment.

### 1.4 JUDGE-PANEL RUBRIC (how Sprint 1 picks the winner)

Score each route 1–5 on each axis; weighted total picks the winner. **Tie → pick the more achievable route (C > A > B on risk).**

| Criterion | Weight | What a 5 looks like |
|---|---|---|
| **Distinctiveness** | 25% | Does not read as the old site, nor as generic dark-SaaS. A stranger would not confuse it with a template. |
| **Fit to positioning** ("builders for Indian industry") | 25% | Communicates engineered competence + scale; not whimsical, not mystical. The 9 industry agents and CustomsIQ feel native to the aesthetic. |
| **Achievability by coding agents (no 3D artist)** | 25% | Showpieces are buildable from procedural/shader/type/SVG within draw budgets. No route depends on hand-authored 3D models, rigged characters, or bespoke illustration. |
| **Mobile degradability** | 15% | Each act has an obvious, non-blank mobile form. No act is "only good with WebGL." |
| **Execution confidence at sprint pace** | 10% | The styleframe slice already looks finished, not 60%. Few flaky dependencies. |

**Build artifacts of Sprint 1:** each candidate route is authored as its own file under `plans/design/candidates/` — `route-A-blueprint.md`, `route-B-genesis.md`, `route-C-kinetic.md` (path form `plans/design/candidates/route-{A,B,C}-<name>.md`). Per-candidate styleframes (the rendered slices the judge reviews) go to `plans/design/styleframes/`.

**Output of the judge step:** the verdict is written to `plans/design/JUDGE.md` — a one-paragraph verdict + the chosen route id (A/B/C). The chosen route's **final locked palette tokens and font choices** are written into `plans/design/TOKENS.md` (the FIRST_LIGHT_CONTRACTS §1 pattern — Tailwind token table + CSS vars + type scale). Everything downstream reads `plans/design/TOKENS.md`.

---

## 2. MULTI-PAGE IA (LOCKED — route names, content mapping, nothing dropped)

> The identifiers in this section (route names, person names, LinkedIn slugs, stat numbers, integration IDs, links) are the design-side restatement of the facts contract. The canonical machine copy is `plans/00-facts/facts.json` (human mirror `plans/00-facts/FACTS.md`); on any conflict, `facts.json` wins and this table must be corrected to match.

React Router (`react-router-dom`, **NEW dep**) with **4 routes**. GitHub Pages SPA fallback is mandatory: `public/404.html` redirect-trick (sessionStorage round-trip, the spa-github-pages pattern) + the SPA-restore snippet in `index.html`; **keep CNAME at repo root AND `public/CNAME`**; `BrowserRouter` (not Hash) since the 404 trick gives us clean URLs on Pages.

| Route | Path | Purpose | Content/facts that land here (ALL must survive) |
|---|---|---|---|
| **Home** | `/` | The cinematic pitch. The 90-second "we make autonomous AI real" story. | Hero (line **"All Is. Now"**, dual CTA: Calendly + "See our work"); **CustomsIQ pipeline showpiece** (Read→Ground→Reason→Classify→Cite, the 9 knowledge-graph nodes); LogoBar **8 industries** (Logistics, Legal Tech, Healthcare, Finance, Agriculture, E-commerce, Manufacturing, Education); **3 Services** (Agentic AI Workflows, Vertical SaaS Products, AI Strategy & Scoping); CtaBanner; **3 Testimonials** (CTO Fintech; Operations Head Logistics; Founder Legal Tech); footer. |
| **Work** | `/work` | Proof. Depth on what we've shipped + who we serve. | **2 Case Studies** (First-Contact Agentic Legal Document Generator — 4–6 hrs → under 10 min; Local Agentic AI Data Analyst for SMEs); **9 Industry Solutions** with before/after + stats (Manufacturing 98% QC, Legal Services 10x throughput, Customs Broking 99.3% classification, FinTech real-time fraud, Enterprise Mgmt 60+ processes, Real Estate Marketing 240% listing exposure, HR Tech 90% screening, Codebase Migration full migration docs — that's 8 visible; preserve the 9th from source). |
| **Studio** | `/studio` | Who builds it + how. The "builders" identity. | **WorkingProcess 4 steps** (Consultation/Research/Strategy → Build Complete → Ship & Measure → Feedback & Iterate); **Team 18 members** with LinkedIn URLs + initials-fallback avatars (Sachmeet Singh Bhatia, Priyank Verma, Astha Porwal, Shovan Mohapatra, Karuna Patil, Abhimanyu Kaundal, Anshul Bharti, Akansha Sarkar, Aditya Gupta, Rishi Mehta, Rakshit Baveja, Poonam Pandey, Shubh Bharadwaj, Shubhankar Anuragi, Rahul Tiwari, Sakshi Joshi, Nipun Katyal, Yugpratap Singh Pawar). |
| **Contact** | `/contact` | Convert. | EmailJS form (`service_gtn59h8` / `template_2l3bp6f` / key `kmCIQtGGQzq1RfE0w`); `hello@kartar.ai`; Calendly `calendly.com/sachmeet-kartar/30min`. |

**Global (every route):** Navbar (links: Home, Work, Studio, Contact — **single source of truth, import from `src/data/nav.js`**; kills the old Navbar/Footer duplication), Footer (Sender.net newsletter form id `axkAXn`, account `08a8ce8a190275`; LinkedIn company link; **Calendly URL must come from one `src/data/links.js` constant** — it appears in 4 places today, dedupe to one). Preloader plays once on first load only (sessionStorage gate), NOT per route.

**De-duplication mandate:** create `src/data/` (new — repo currently has zero data files): `nav.js`, `links.js` (Calendly, email, LinkedIn, Sender ids, EmailJS ids), `team.js`, `industries.js`, `caseStudies.js`, `services.js`, `process.js`, `testimonials.js`, `pipeline.js`. Sprint sections import from these. This is the FACTS CONTRACT made executable.

**Page-transition concept (LOCKED mechanism, route-skin per winner):** a **chapter-wipe** transition. On route change, a full-bleed overlay (the route's signature material — blueprint paper for A, particle dissolve for B, ink-block for C) sweeps **horizontally** across the viewport, covers, swaps content at cover-peak, reveals on the way out, total ≤ 700ms. Implemented with framer-motion `AnimatePresence` on a route wrapper + a single GSAP timeline for the wipe; scroll resets to top *under cover* (never a visible jump). Reduced-motion = instant cross-fade ≤ 200ms, no wipe.

---

## 3. ACT-BY-ACT EXPERIENCE STORYBOARD

Direction-of-travel is enforced. Each act names the technique + the resource that implements it. **★ = WebGL showpiece** (max 3 total, all on Home/Work; Studio + Contact are DOM/GSAP only).

### ROUTE: Home (`/`)

The hero's signature object idles (route-dependent: blueprint cube wireframe / particle nucleus / kinetic type slab). Background parallaxes opposite to content. | content settles in place; bg drifts **diagonally** counter to scroll | Kinetic type via SplitText; `drei ScrollControls` OR scoped R3F canvas for the idle object (lazy, hero-only, as today). |

Scroll back = it disassembles in reverse. This is the Apple-AirPods assemble scrub, rebuilt: **nodes are the "parts," edges draw like wiring, the final HS code is the "assembled product."** | nodes converge from **all four edges → center** (centripetal); the graph itself is the still point while inputs travel | **★ R3F instanced points/lines** (nodes = instanced meshes, edges = `Line2`/`LineSegments`, labels = drei `Html` or sprite text) driven by a single pinned **GSAP ScrollTrigger scrub** (NOT the old hand-rolled 480vh black box — replace it). | H3 | **Industries marquee (8)** | The 8 industry words run as a **horizontal kinetic marquee**, speed coupled to scroll velocity; hovering/centering a word lights it. | **horizontal**, velocity-reactive | `xPercent` marquee on gsap.ticker + scrollBus velocity (BellowsMarquee mechanics, no skew except Candidate C). Resource: gsap.com/scroll velocity demos. |
| H4 | **Services (3)** | Three service cards **stack and lock** like dealt cards / sheets onto a table; each card's spec text engraves in. As you scroll the stack advances. | cards arrive **from the right, stack with slight rotation** (card physics) | Card-physics stacking (StickyStack reauthored) + magnetic CTA. Resource: aceternity/reactbits card-stack patterns; existing `StickyStack.jsx`. |

| H6 | **CTA Banner** | Big line + magnetic primary button (Calendly). Background material reacts to cursor. | type scales **up in depth**; cursor-magnetic | MagneticButton (existing, retuned heavier) + SplitText. |

### ROUTE: Work (`/work`)

Resource: demos.gsap.com horizontal pin. Scar #1: defer any self-kill with `gsap.delayedCall(0,…)`. |

Optional shader-reveal: each card's "after" image dissolves in via displacement. | volumes **rise (depth/up) while the field pans horizontally** under pin | **★ R3F single instanced draw for 9 volumes** (per-instance extrude + heat via instanceMatrix/instanceColor — never 9 meshes; FIRST_LIGHT §3 Skyline discipline) OR, if winner is Candidate C, a **DOM/GSAP card-grid with Codrops R3F image-reveal shader** only on the hero image of each. ## 4. ASSET STRATEGY (no 3D artist, no paid assets)

- **CustomsIQ graph (H2):** pure procedural — nodes are instanced low-poly spheres/rounded quads, edges are `Line2`/`LineSegments`, labels are drei `Html` (DOM, crisp text, no font-in-GL) or MSDF text via `troika-three-text` only if `Html` causes z-fighting. Node positions come straight from `src/data/pipeline.js` (the existing `{id,x,y,t,step}` table, re-spaced for 3D). **Zero external models.**
- **Skyline (W2):** one instanced geometry (box/rounded-box), per-instance transform + color. If image-reveal is used, the "after" images are **generated by code** (canvas-drawn stat cards / SVG-rasterized), not photography.
- **Hero object (H1):** procedural — wireframe polyhedron (Candidate A), GPU particle nucleus (B), or extruded type slab (C). All shader/geometry, no assets.
- **Image sequences:** only if code-generatable. Prefer live R3F scrub over pre-rendered frames. If a sequence is unavoidable, generate frames programmatically (canvas) at build time, ≤ 60 frames, WebP, ≤ 1280px long edge.
- **Typography-as-object:** giant type IS the art on Candidate C; SVG strokes are the art on Candidate A.
- **Fonts — self-hosting plan:** download chosen families from Fontshare/Google as **WOFF2 only**, place in `public/fonts/`, `@font-face` with `font-display: swap`, subset to Latin + the few glyphs used in mono labels. Budget: ≤ 4 font files, ≤ 220KB total. No Google Fonts CDN link (offline-safe, no layout-shift from network). **All motion that splits/measures text waits on `document.fonts.ready`** (scar: injected styles + late fonts cause re-measure jank).
- **Byte budgets (transfer, gzipped):**
  - Initial Home route (no GL yet): **JS ≤ 180KB**, CSS ≤ 30KB, fonts ≤ 220KB.
  - `three` + `@react-three/fiber` + `drei` chunk: **lazy, ≤ 320KB gzip**, fetched only on `full`/`mid` tier and only when a showpiece section nears viewport.
  - `@react-three/rapier` (**NEW dep**): only if a showpiece needs physics; gated behind the showpiece's own `lazy()` and `tier==="full"`. If H2/W2 don't need true physics (they don't — scrub-driven transforms suffice), **do not add rapier at all.** Default: no rapier.
  - Per-showpiece lazy chunk ≤ 120KB on top of the shared GL chunk.
  - Total page transfer on `full` tier ≤ 1.1MB; on `lite`/mobile ≤ 420KB (zero three bytes).

---

## 5. MOTION DOCTRINE (the anti-flaky laws — adapted from FIRST_LIGHT)

### 5.1 The laws (LOCKED)

1. **One easing language.** Define once in `src/lib/motion.js`: a single `PRIMARY` cubic-bezier for entrances/strikes and `SETTLE = "expo.out"` for cooling/settling. Register a GSAP `CustomEase` to match. No ad-hoc beziers in section files.
2. **Scrub over trigger.** Anything tied to scroll position uses a scrubbed ScrollTrigger. Trigger-once is reserved for "strike" entrances, which then **HOLD their end state forever** (the "forged-stays-forged" law: no re-trigger, no fade-out, no reverse on scroll-up except for the explicitly reversible showpieces H2/W1/W2).
3. **High-water latch.** Reveal progress only grows (latched), so fast scrolls never leave an act half-built. Scrub-latch triggers MUST have `onLeave`/`onLeaveBack` latch (scar #5).
4. **Velocity awareness.** Marquees and skews read a single shared `scrollBus.velocity`; one writer, many readers. Velocity decays to zero within ~1.5s of rest.
5. **No standalone rAF loops.** Lenis is raf-driven from `gsap.ticker` (current architecture). All per-frame work hangs off `gsap.ticker` or `useFrame`. **Never** a bare `requestAnimationFrame` loop.
6. **Zero per-frame allocations.** Module-level scratch objects, pre-allocated typed arrays in `useMemo`, mutate-never-replace for vectors/colors (`.set()`), `dt = Math.min(delta, 0.05)`.
7. **No per-frame `getBoundingClientRect`.** Measure on `ScrollTrigger.refresh`/resize only; cache rects.
8. **ScrollTrigger scopes the REAL window.** Lenis scrolls the real window (NO scrollerProxy today). **Do NOT move scroll into a transformed wrapper** — it silently breaks every pin (scar #2). Page-transition wipes are overlays, not scroll containers.
9. **Never `self.kill()` synchronously inside `onUpdate`** — corrupts trigger iteration and silently freezes later triggers. Defer: `gsap.delayedCall(0, () => trigger.kill())` (scar #1).
10. **Coordinated refresh.** Call `ScrollTrigger.refresh()` once after fonts ready + images decoded + preloader done; never per-section uncoordinated refreshes (scar #3).
11. **`position: relative`** on any section hosting an `absolute inset-0` overlay/veil (scar #4).
12. **Injected `<style>` outranks Tailwind** — prefer Tailwind utilities; if you must inject, scope it and know it wins the cascade (scar #6).
13. **StrictMode-idempotent.** Every imperative effect must be safe under double-invoke; use `gsap.context()` with revert cleanup (scar #7).
14. **Minimums.** Nothing animates under 0.3s. Minimum perceptible move = 40px or a full material/state change. No sub-pixel fidgets.

### 5.2 Multi-directional doctrine (LOCKED)

- Every act declares a **dominant travel vector** that is not plain vertical-with-scroll. Allowed: left, right, diagonal, depth (z), centripetal, counter-parallax. At least **3 acts per route** must move horizontally or in depth.
- Background and foreground must travel at different rates and ideally **opposite directions** (counter-parallax) so the eye never reads "a tall column scrolling up."
- Pinned acts (H2, W1, W2, S1) convert vertical scroll into lateral/assembly motion — this is the core "page is not scrolling, things are moving" mechanism.

### 5.3 Reduced-motion / mobile contract (LOCKED — never blank)

Central gating stays in `src/lib/useReducedMotion.js` (breakpoint 768). `prefersReducedMotion() || isMobileViewport()` resolves a **tier** (port the FIRST_LIGHT tier matrix: `full / mid / lite / none`). What each technique degrades to:

| Technique | Mobile / `lite` | Reduced-motion / `none` |
|---|---|---|
| H2 CustomsIQ 3D graph | **2D SVG/Canvas** version of the same graph, scrubbed (the old DOM graph is the lite design) — never the WebGL path | Static finished graph (all nodes lit, HS code centered), no scrub |
| W2 skyline extrude | DOM CSS card grid, GSAP reveal, no canvas | Static card grid, before/after both shown, stats as final numbers |
| H1 hero object | CSS/SVG poster of the object (existing OrbFallback pattern) | Static poster |
| Horizontal pins (H5/W1/S1) | **Native vertical stack** (no pin), each panel a normal section | Static stack |
| Kinetic marquees | Slow CSS marquee or static word list | Static list |
| Card-physics stack (H4) | Plain stacked cards, simple fade-up | Static cards |
| Magnetic cursor/buttons | Disabled (touch); normal tap targets | Disabled |
| Page-transition wipe | Cross-fade ≤ 200ms | Instant, no transition |
| CountUp / count-strike | Final number rendered immediately | Final number |

**Mobile must never load any `three` byte.** `lite`/`none` render zero WebGL chunks (QA asserts no GL chunk fetched on those tiers — port FIRST_LIGHT `verify.mjs` chunk-leak check).

---

## 6. LOCKED vs SPRINT-1-DECIDES

### LOCKED HERE (do not change downstream)
- **IA & route names:** `/` Home, `/work` Work, `/studio` Studio, `/contact` Contact.
- **Content mapping** (§2 table) — which facts live on which route; nothing dropped.
- **Facts contract** (§2) — all names/numbers/links/integration IDs verbatim; `src/data/` single-source files; canonical machine copy `plans/00-facts/facts.json`.
- **Showpiece list** (§3 ledger): exactly 3 WebGL moments; H2 (CustomsIQ assemble/disassemble) and W2 (skyline) are mandatory; H1 may downgrade to CSS/SVG.
- **Page-transition mechanism:** horizontal chapter-wipe ≤ 700ms via AnimatePresence + GSAP, scroll-reset under cover.
- **Motion doctrine** (§5) — all 14 laws, multi-directional doctrine, the 4-tier degrade matrix, the reduced-motion contract.
- **Asset constraints & byte budgets** (§4).
- **Stack additions:** `react-router-dom` (new), GitHub Pages SPA fallback (404.html trick + CNAME in root and public). Rapier only if a showpiece truly needs physics (default: not added).
- **Three competing routes + judge rubric** are how the winner is chosen.
- **Scars** (§5.1 laws 8/9/10/11/13) are protocol, not suggestions.

### SPRINT 1 DECIDES (and writes to `plans/design/TOKENS.md`)
- **Winning creative direction:** A (Blueprint→Built), B (Genesis), or C (Kinetic Index), via the §1.4 rubric.
- **Final palette tokens** (the Tailwind token table + CSS vars) — within the winner's stated direction and the forbidden-color constraint.
- **Final type tokens:** display + body + mono families (from the winner's named candidates; Fontshare/Google only; self-hosted WOFF2) + the type scale (hero clamp, head clamp, body size/leading, mono tracking).
- **Exact easing constants** (the cubic-bezier numbers) and durations, within the §5.1 minimums.
- **Hero object form** (H1) and whether it stays WebGL or downgrades.
- **Whether W2 uses the R3F image-reveal shader** or stays instanced-extrude (depends on winner; C → DOM+shader, A/B → instanced).

---

## 7. PORT THESE FROM `feat/first-light` (don't reinvent)

- **`scripts/qa/`** harness — `verify.mjs` (~27 DOM/console/integration/chunk-leak assertions against dev server :5173; **add per-route assertions** for the 4 routes + the 404 SPA-fallback + EmailJS POST shape + no-GL-on-lite), `walkthrough.mjs` + `dense_walk.mjs` (screenshot walks to `/tmp/shots` — **walk all 4 routes + a page transition**), `content_diff.py` (repurpose from byte-identity to **facts-presence** check against `src/data/*`), `README.md`. Read via `git show feat/first-light:scripts/qa/verify.mjs` etc.
- **The CONTRACTS-doc pattern itself** — `FIRST_LIGHT_CONTRACTS.md` is the model for the new build's coordination doc. Sprint 0/1 should produce `plans/design/BUILD_CONTRACTS.md` in that shape (token table, tier matrix, per-route DOM contract, locked functional contracts, anti-flaky doctrine) so every later sprint reads one binding interface.
- **Salvage on disk now:** `src/components/motion/` primitives (Reveal, SplitText, MagneticButton, SpotlightCard, StickyStack, HorizontalScroll, ScrollScrubSequence/Video, CountUp, Section), `src/lib/useReducedMotion.js` (breakpoint 768), `src/lib/motion.js` tokens, `src/lib/SmoothScroll.jsx` (Lenis-from-gsap.ticker). Reauthor feel, keep mechanics. Delete dead deps (react-scroll, split-type, @gsap/react) unless a primitive still needs them.
- **Known-fragile to REPLACE, not salvage:** the hand-rolled non-GSAP 480vh `ScrollScrubShowcase` — rebuild as the H2 GSAP-scrubbed showpiece.

### Critical Files for Implementation
- /Users/prynq/Desktop/kartar/sachmeetsb.github.io/src/components/ScrollScrubShowcase.jsx (the CustomsIQ pipeline data `{id,x,y,t,step}` + 5 stages; the black-box to replace with the H2 showpiece — source of truth for `src/data/pipeline.js`)

export const meta = {
  name: 'sprint01-styleframes',
  description: 'Build 3 candidate art-direction styleframes (HTML) + candidate spec docs in parallel',
  phases: [{ title: 'Build', detail: 'one agent per candidate route A/B/C' }],
}

const ROOT = '/Users/prynq/Desktop/kartar/sachmeetsb.github.io'

const SHARED = `
You are a world-class web art director + front-end craftsman building ONE candidate art-direction styleframe for the rebrand of kartar.ai ("Kartar AI Labs" — builders of autonomous AI for Indian industry). Brand meaning: Kartar = creator/builder/the one who makes. Feeling to sell: COMPETENCE THAT MAKES THINGS REAL. Not mysticism, not generic dark-SaaS gradients, not a template.

This frame will be screenshotted at 1440x900 (viewport) AND full-page, then scored by a hostile 3-critic judge panel against two competitors. The judge scores PIXELS, not prose. Make it look ~FINISHED, gallery-grade — not 60%.

== DELIVERABLE 1: the styleframe ==
Write ONE standalone self-contained HTML file (you will be told the exact path). Rules:
- Inline ALL CSS in a <style> tag. Optional minimal inline JS and inline SVG allowed. NO build step, NO local imports — must open via file://.
- Fonts: you MAY use Google Fonts CDN (<link href="https://fonts.googleapis.com/...">) and/or Fontshare CDN (https://api.fontshare.com/v2/css?f[]=clash-display@600,700&f[]=satoshi@400,500 style URLs). This is a throwaway screenshot artifact; CDN is fine here only.
- Design width 1440. Total page height roughly 1800–2200px: it is a vertical slice of the Home route = above-the-fold HERO + the FIRST SCROLL ACT.
- The screenshot is a STILL taken ~2.5s after load. Avoid mid-animation states: either no animation, or CSS animations that fully settle within 1.5s, or design looping ambient motion whose every frame reads as composed. Nothing may be at opacity<1 or translated off-position at the 2.5s mark.
- Content the frame MUST visibly render (real content, never lorem):
  1. A nav strip: wordmark "kartarAI" (the "AI" may be treated as a distinct mark) + links Home / Work / Studio / Contact + a CTA "Book a Call".
  2. HERO: the brand line "All Is. Now" must appear (typographically re-set however you like, words exact). A headline carrying the meaning "Some industries haven't changed in 30 years. We're changing them now." (you may rewrite the wording in your direction's voice; keep the meaning). A short subline about agentic AI systems for Indian businesses. DUAL CTA: "Book a Call" (primary) + "See our work" (secondary). A signature hero visual per your route spec below.
  3. FIRST ACT — the CustomsIQ pipeline aesthetic hint (static is fine): must show the 5 stage names Read / Ground / Reason / Classify / Cite as the visible vocabulary, plus a terminal/trace or knowledge-graph flavor using these REAL numbers verbatim: "HS 8471.30", "BCD 0%", "IGST 18%", "₹2,41,800", "confidence 0.94". Panels may be titled "agent.trace" and "knowledge graph · HS 8471". Render it in YOUR route's material language.
  4. Somewhere in the slice: the 8-industries strip — Logistics, Legal Tech, Healthcare, Finance, Agriculture, E-commerce, Manufacturing, Education (e.g. a marquee row, an index list — per your route).
- FORBIDDEN as primary/dominant colors (old brand — instant disqualification): saffron #F2A93B, indigo #2D1B69 / #4A2F9A, teal #00BFA5, ember #FF5E0E. The page must NOT read as "the old site recolored". Grounds are neutral (graphite/bone per your spec); ONE saturated accent at <=10% of any frame.
- Craft bar: deliberate grid, real typographic hierarchy (eyebrow → display → body → mono labels), generous negative space, consistent spacing rhythm, designed corners/details (dimension marks, node dots, index numbers — per route). Contrast must be high: body text clearly legible against its ground. No default-Tailwind look, no centered-everything genericism.

== DELIVERABLE 2: the candidate spec doc ==
Write a markdown spec (you will be told the exact path) with EXACTLY these sections:
# Candidate <ID> — <NAME>
## Mood            (one sentence)
## Palette         (a table of concrete hex values + role: ground / surface / ink / accent / muted — must match the HTML and respect the forbidden list; state accent coverage <=10%)
## Type            (Display + Body + Mono families — the named options for your route only, all Fontshare/Google self-hostable WOFF2; name intended weights)
## Motion thesis   (ONE sentence "what is moving", then the dominant travel vectors used — must be multi-directional: >=3 acts horizontal/depth/centripetal/counter-parallax)
## Signature WebGL moments  (2–3, referencing the LOCKED ledger: H2 CustomsIQ assemble/disassemble from screen edges→center [mandatory], W2 industry-skyline extrude [mandatory], H1 hero idle object [may downgrade to CSS/SVG] — describe each in YOUR route's material language + its mobile/reduced degrade)
## Page IA         (restate LOCKED: routes / /work /studio /contact; Home order H0 preloader → H1 hero → H2 pipeline → H3 industries marquee → H4 services x3 → H5 testimonials x3 → H6 CTA)
## Reference anchors (which of: obys.agency, ten.375.studio, apple.com/airpods scrub, lusion.co, Codrops R3F image-reveal, cuberto.com, storytelling.noomoagency.com — and what is borrowed)

Return (as your final text): a one-paragraph summary of the design moves you made + both file paths written. Do not run git. Do not touch any other file.
`

const CANDIDATES = [
  {
    id: 'A', slug: 'blueprint',
    html: `${ROOT}/plans/design/styleframes/route-A.html`,
    spec: `${ROOT}/plans/design/candidates/route-A-blueprint.md`,
    brief: `
== YOUR ROUTE: Candidate A — "BLUEPRINT → BUILT" (atelier / drafting-table) ==
Concept: the site is a drafting table where blueprints resolve into built reality. Ghost wireframe linework, dimension marks, annotations exist first; as you move they INK IN, fill with material, snap into finished objects. The most on-brand for "builders".
- Mood: precise, confident, engineered. Quiet luxury of a well-made tool. Technical drawing meets product photography.
- Palette: near-black graphite ground (~#0C0D10), warm paper/bone (~#EDE7DA) as the "built/inked" surface, ONE electric structural accent = the live blueprint line: choose cyan-leaning blueprint blue (~#3B7CFF) OR acid lime (~#C8FF3D) — pick ONE and commit. Greys do the heavy lifting; accent <=10% of frame.
- Type: Display = Clash Display (Fontshare; geometric — NOT a serif). Body = Geist (Google) or Satoshi (Fontshare). Mono (dimension marks, spec-sheet voice) = JetBrains Mono or IBM Plex Mono.
- Motion personality (express as STILL aesthetics + spec prose): mechanical precision, cubic-bezier(.7,0,.2,1), short authoritative 0.5–0.7s moves, snap-to-grid with slight overshoot, stroke-dashoffset line draws. Weighty, never bouncy.
- Visual vocabulary for the frame: visible drafting grid / graph-paper field, dimension lines with arrowheads + mono measurements annotating the headline ("ALL IS. NOW" with width/height callouts like a technical drawing), a hero object as a wireframe-to-solid polyhedron or isometric blueprint of the knowledge graph, half-inked/half-wireframe treatments, title-block corner stamps (like engineering drawings: "KARTAR AI LABS · DWG 001 · REV 2026"), crop marks, registration ticks.
- The CustomsIQ act: render as a blueprint sheet — the knowledge graph as a technical schematic, nodes as drafted circles with leader-line labels, the agent.trace as a spec table.
- Borrows: obys.agency (kinetic technical type + grid), ten.375.studio (ruler/axis nav), apple.com/airpods (assemble-from-parts).`,
  },
  {
    id: 'B', slug: 'genesis',
    html: `${ROOT}/plans/design/styleframes/route-B.html`,
    spec: `${ROOT}/plans/design/candidates/route-B-genesis.md`,
    brief: `
== YOUR ROUTE: Candidate B — "GENESIS / RAW MATTER" (act-of-creation) ==
Concept: the site is formless matter that organizes into product as you move. Particle fields, point clouds, noise coalesce into legible objects (a logo, a knowledge graph, a product) then disperse. Most spectacular, highest WebGL ambition — the showpieces carry it.
- Mood: cosmic-but-controlled, awe, emergence. Dark, deep, light BORN from motion.
- Palette: deep space charcoal/black ground, cool off-white matter, and ONE two-tone energy gradient as the only color — duotone NOT in the forbidden list: e.g. violet→electric-blue (#7C5CFF → #2FD0FF) or magenta→amber-white. Energy color appears ONLY where motion/coalescence happens; page mostly monochrome with bursts.
- Type: Display = Boska (Fontshare) or Hanken Grotesk wide/heavy. Body = Hanken Grotesk or Geist. Mono = Space Mono. Big, airy, lots of negative space so the particle moments breathe.
- Motion personality (express as STILL aesthetics + spec prose): organic, eased, settles 1.0–1.4s, expo.out coalescing, mass-and-drift particle physics. Things GATHER rather than slide.
- Visual vocabulary for the frame: a particle nucleus / point-cloud hero object mid-coalescence (render with inline SVG circles or canvas-drawn dots — hundreds of dots with density gradient, gathering into a recognizable form near the headline), scattered→ordered dot fields as section dividers, faint constellation lines, the energy gradient only on the coalescing front and CTA.
- The CustomsIQ act: the knowledge graph as a constellation being born — nodes as gathered bright points, edges as faint light lines, stage names as waypoints along the coalescence; the agent.trace panel as dark glass with glowing mono text.
- Borrows: lusion.co (progressive reveal pacing, depth), Codrops R3F image-reveal (matter→image), storytelling.noomoagency.com (chaptered emergence).`,
  },
  {
    id: 'C', slug: 'kinetic-index',
    html: `${ROOT}/plans/design/styleframes/route-C.html`,
    spec: `${ROOT}/plans/design/candidates/route-C-kinetic-index.md`,
    brief: `
== YOUR ROUTE: Candidate C — "KINETIC INDEX" (editorial / type-as-machine) ==
Concept: a kinetic editorial system where GIANT TYPOGRAPHY is the primary moving object and 3D is sparse punctuation. Horizontal pinned chapters, magnetic cursor, type traveling on its own axis. Lowest WebGL risk, highest Awwwards-typographic payoff, most mobile-robust.
- Mood: loud, confident, design-forward studio. Swiss-grid-broken-on-purpose. Editorial.
- Palette: LIGHT MODE DOMINANT — bone/paper ground (~#F4F1EA) with near-black ink — deliberately inverts the dark-SaaS expectation (both prior rejected attempts were dark; light IS the differentiator). Plus ONE hot accent: oxblood (~#C8472E) or electric chartreuse — pick ONE and commit. Accent <=10%.
- Type: this route lives or dies on type. Display = Clash Display (Fontshare) PLUS one contrasting expressive serif accent = Editorial-style: Gambetta (Fontshare) — mix the two display faces deliberately (e.g. serif italic for "Now" or for editorial asides). Body = Satoshi. Mono = JetBrains Mono.
- Motion personality (express as STILL aesthetics + spec prose): snappy, kinetic, horizontal; magnetic cursor; type overshoots and settles; xPercent marquees coupled to scroll velocity; skew-on-velocity (the ONE allowed skew). Fast 0.4–0.6s.
- Visual vocabulary for the frame: oversized display type as the hero object (the headline IS the visual — words at 120–200px, cropped/bleeding off-edge on purpose), an index/table-of-contents aesthetic (numbered rows 01–04 for routes or acts, hairline rules), a giant horizontal industries marquee row mid-frame (8 industries, one frozen mid-travel with a word highlighted in the accent), editorial serif italic punctuation, tight mono metadata labels (coordinates, indexes, timestamps).
- The CustomsIQ act: editorial spread — huge "READ → GROUND → REASON → CLASSIFY → CITE" as an index list or running header band, the trace as a typeset specimen table with the real numbers, knowledge graph as a minimal black-ink diagram with one accent node.
- Borrows: obys.agency (kinetic technical type), cuberto.com (magnetic cursor), ten.375.studio (axis nav); the single 3D moment is the Apple-style assemble showpiece.`,
  },
]

phase('Build')
log('Building 3 candidate styleframes in parallel (A blueprint / B genesis / C kinetic-index)')
const results = await parallel(CANDIDATES.map(c => () =>
  agent(
    SHARED +
    c.brief +
    `\n\n== EXACT OUTPUT PATHS ==\n- styleframe HTML: ${c.html}\n- candidate spec doc: ${c.spec}\nCreate both files now. The directories may not exist yet — Write will create them.`,
    { label: `build:route-${c.id}`, phase: 'Build' }
  )
))
return results.map((r, i) => ({ route: CANDIDATES[i].id, summary: r }))

This frame will be screenshotted at 1440x900 (viewport) AND full-page, then scored by a hostile 3-critic judge panel against two competitors. The judge scores PIXELS, not prose. Make it look ~FINISHED, gallery-grade — not 60%.

== DELIVERABLE 1: the styleframe ==
Write ONE standalone self-contained HTML file (you will be told the exact path). Rules:
- Inline ALL CSS in a <style> tag. Optional minimal inline JS and inline SVG allowed. NO build step, NO local imports — must open via file://.
- Fonts: you MAY use Google Fonts CDN (<link href="https://fonts.googleapis.com/...">) and/or Fontshare CDN (https://api.fontshare.com/v2/css?f[]=clash-display@600,700&f[]=satoshi@400,500 style URLs). This is a throwaway screenshot artifact; CDN is fine here only.
- Design width 1440. Total page height roughly 1800–2200px: it is a vertical slice of the Home route = above-the-fold HERO + the FIRST SCROLL ACT.
- The screenshot is a STILL taken ~2.5s after load. Avoid mid-animation states: either no animation, or CSS animations that fully settle within 1.5s, or design looping ambient motion whose every frame reads as composed. Nothing may be at opacity<1 or translated off-position at the 2.5s mark.
- Content the frame MUST visibly render (real content, never lorem):
  1. A nav strip: wordmark "kartarAI" (the "AI" may be treated as a distinct mark) + links Home / Work / Studio / Contact + a CTA "Book a Call".
  2. HERO: the brand line "All Is. Now" must appear (typographically re-set however you like, words exact). A headline carrying the meaning "Some industries haven't changed in 30 years. We're changing them now." (you may rewrite the wording in your direction's voice; keep the meaning). A short subline about agentic AI systems for Indian businesses. DUAL CTA: "Book a Call" (primary) + "See our work" (secondary). A signature hero visual per your route spec below.
  3. FIRST ACT — the CustomsIQ pipeline aesthetic hint (static is fine): must show the 5 stage names Read / Ground / Reason / Classify / Cite as the visible vocabulary, plus a terminal/trace or knowledge-graph flavor using these REAL numbers verbatim: "HS 8471.30", "BCD 0%", "IGST 18%", "₹2,41,800", "confidence 0.94". Panels may be titled "agent.trace" and "knowledge graph · HS 8471". Render it in YOUR route's material language.
  4. Somewhere in the slice: the 8-industries strip — Logistics, Legal Tech, Healthcare, Finance, Agriculture, E-commerce, Manufacturing, Education (e.g. a marquee row, an index list — per your route).
- FORBIDDEN as primary/dominant colors (old brand — instant disqualification): saffron #F2A93B, indigo #2D1B69 / #4A2F9A, teal #00BFA5, ember #FF5E0E. The page must NOT read as "the old site recolored". Grounds are neutral (graphite/bone per your spec); ONE saturated accent at <=10% of any frame.
- Craft bar: deliberate grid, real typographic hierarchy (eyebrow → display → body → mono labels), generous negative space, consistent spacing rhythm, designed corners/details (dimension marks, node dots, index numbers — per route). Contrast must be high: body text clearly legible against its ground. No default-Tailwind look, no centered-everything genericism.

== DELIVERABLE 2: the candidate spec doc ==
Write a markdown spec (you will be told the exact path) with EXACTLY these sections:
# Candidate <ID> — <NAME>
## Mood            (one sentence)
## Palette         (a table of concrete hex values + role: ground / surface / ink / accent / muted — must match the HTML and respect the forbidden list; state accent coverage <=10%)
## Type            (Display + Body + Mono families — the named options for your route only, all Fontshare/Google self-hostable WOFF2; name intended weights)
## Motion thesis   (ONE sentence "what is moving", then the dominant travel vectors used — must be multi-directional: >=3 acts horizontal/depth/centripetal/counter-parallax)
## Signature WebGL moments  (2–3, referencing the LOCKED ledger: H2 CustomsIQ assemble/disassemble from screen edges→center [mandatory], W2 industry-skyline extrude [mandatory], H1 hero idle object [may downgrade to CSS/SVG] — describe each in YOUR route's material language + its mobile/reduced degrade)
## Page IA         (restate LOCKED: routes / /work /studio /contact; Home order H0 preloader → H1 hero → H2 pipeline → H3 industries marquee → H4 services x3 → H5 testimonials x3 → H6 CTA)
## Reference anchors (which of: obys.agency, ten.375.studio, apple.com/airpods scrub, lusion.co, Codrops R3F image-reveal, cuberto.com, storytelling.noomoagency.com — and what is borrowed)

Return (as your final text): a one-paragraph summary of the design moves you made + both file paths written. Do not run git. Do not touch any other file.
`

const CANDIDATES = [
  {
    id: 'A', slug: 'blueprint',
    html: `${ROOT}/plans/design/styleframes/route-A.html`,
    spec: `${ROOT}/plans/design/candidates/route-A-blueprint.md`,
    brief: `
== YOUR ROUTE: Candidate A — "BLUEPRINT → BUILT" (atelier / drafting-table) ==
Concept: the site is a drafting table where blueprints resolve into built reality. Ghost wireframe linework, dimension marks, annotations exist first; as you move they INK IN, fill with material, snap into finished objects. The most on-brand for "builders".
- Mood: precise, confident, engineered. Quiet luxury of a well-made tool. Technical drawing meets product photography.
- Palette: near-black graphite ground (~#0C0D10), warm paper/bone (~#EDE7DA) as the "built/inked" surface, ONE electric structural accent = the live blueprint line: choose cyan-leaning blueprint blue (~#3B7CFF) OR acid lime (~#C8FF3D) — pick ONE and commit. Greys do the heavy lifting; accent <=10% of frame.
- Type: Display = Clash Display (Fontshare; geometric — NOT a serif). Body = Geist (Google) or Satoshi (Fontshare). Mono (dimension marks, spec-sheet voice) = JetBrains Mono or IBM Plex Mono.
- Motion personality (express as STILL aesthetics + spec prose): mechanical precision, cubic-bezier(.7,0,.2,1), short authoritative 0.5–0.7s moves, snap-to-grid with slight overshoot, stroke-dashoffset line draws. Weighty, never bouncy.
- Visual vocabulary for the frame: visible drafting grid / graph-paper field, dimension lines with arrowheads + mono measurements annotating the headline ("ALL IS. NOW" with width/height callouts like a technical drawing), a hero object as a wireframe-to-solid polyhedron or isometric blueprint of the knowledge graph, half-inked/half-wireframe treatments, title-block corner stamps (like engineering drawings: "KARTAR AI LABS · DWG 001 · REV 2026"), crop marks, registration ticks.
- The CustomsIQ act: render as a blueprint sheet — the knowledge graph as a technical schematic, nodes as drafted circles with leader-line labels, the agent.trace as a spec table.
- Borrows: obys.agency (kinetic technical type + grid), ten.375.studio (ruler/axis nav), apple.com/airpods (assemble-from-parts).`,
  },
  {
    id: 'B', slug: 'genesis',
    html: `${ROOT}/plans/design/styleframes/route-B.html`,
    spec: `${ROOT}/plans/design/candidates/route-B-genesis.md`,
    brief: `
== YOUR ROUTE: Candidate B — "GENESIS / RAW MATTER" (act-of-creation) ==
Concept: the site is formless matter that organizes into product as you move. Particle fields, point clouds, noise coalesce into legible objects (a logo, a knowledge graph, a product) then disperse. Most spectacular, highest WebGL ambition — the showpieces carry it.
- Mood: cosmic-but-controlled, awe, emergence. Dark, deep, light BORN from motion.
- Palette: deep space charcoal/black ground, cool off-white matter, and ONE two-tone energy gradient as the only color — duotone NOT in the forbidden list: e.g. violet→electric-blue (#7C5CFF → #2FD0FF) or magenta→amber-white. Energy color appears ONLY where motion/coalescence happens; page mostly monochrome with bursts.
- Type: Display = Boska (Fontshare) or Hanken Grotesk wide/heavy. Body = Hanken Grotesk or Geist. Mono = Space Mono. Big, airy, lots of negative space so the particle moments breathe.
- Motion personality (express as STILL aesthetics + spec prose): organic, eased, settles 1.0–1.4s, expo.out coalescing, mass-and-drift particle physics. Things GATHER rather than slide.
- Visual vocabulary for the frame: a particle nucleus / point-cloud hero object mid-coalescence (render with inline SVG circles or canvas-drawn dots — hundreds of dots with density gradient, gathering into a recognizable form near the headline), scattered→ordered dot fields as section dividers, faint constellation lines, the energy gradient only on the coalescing front and CTA.
- The CustomsIQ act: the knowledge graph as a constellation being born — nodes as gathered bright points, edges as faint light lines, stage names as waypoints along the coalescence; the agent.trace panel as dark glass with glowing mono text.
- Borrows: lusion.co (progressive reveal pacing, depth), Codrops R3F image-reveal (matter→image), storytelling.noomoagency.com (chaptered emergence).`,
  },
  {
    id: 'C', slug: 'kinetic-index',
    html: `${ROOT}/plans/design/styleframes/route-C.html`,
    spec: `${ROOT}/plans/design/candidates/route-C-kinetic-index.md`,
    brief: `
== YOUR ROUTE: Candidate C — "KINETIC INDEX" (editorial / type-as-machine) ==
Concept: a kinetic editorial system where GIANT TYPOGRAPHY is the primary moving object and 3D is sparse punctuation. Horizontal pinned chapters, magnetic cursor, type traveling on its own axis. Lowest WebGL risk, highest Awwwards-typographic payoff, most mobile-robust.
- Mood: loud, confident, design-forward studio. Swiss-grid-broken-on-purpose. Editorial.
- Palette: LIGHT MODE DOMINANT — bone/paper ground (~#F4F1EA) with near-black ink — deliberately inverts the dark-SaaS expectation (both prior rejected attempts were dark; light IS the differentiator). Plus ONE hot accent: oxblood (~#C8472E) or electric chartreuse — pick ONE and commit. Accent <=10%.
- Type: this route lives or dies on type. Display = Clash Display (Fontshare) PLUS one contrasting expressive serif accent = Editorial-style: Gambetta (Fontshare) — mix the two display faces deliberately (e.g. serif italic for "Now" or for editorial asides). Body = Satoshi. Mono = JetBrains Mono.
- Motion personality (express as STILL aesthetics + spec prose): snappy, kinetic, horizontal; magnetic cursor; type overshoots and settles; xPercent marquees coupled to scroll velocity; skew-on-velocity (the ONE allowed skew). Fast 0.4–0.6s.
- Visual vocabulary for the frame: oversized display type as the hero object (the headline IS the visual — words at 120–200px, cropped/bleeding off-edge on purpose), an index/table-of-contents aesthetic (numbered rows 01–04 for routes or acts, hairline rules), a giant horizontal industries marquee row mid-frame (8 industries, one frozen mid-travel with a word highlighted in the accent), editorial serif italic punctuation, tight mono metadata labels (coordinates, indexes, timestamps).
- The CustomsIQ act: editorial spread — huge "READ → GROUND → REASON → CLASSIFY → CITE" as an index list or running header band, the trace as a typeset specimen table with the real numbers, knowledge graph as a minimal black-ink diagram with one accent node.
- Borrows: obys.agency (kinetic technical type), cuberto.com (magnetic cursor), ten.375.studio (axis nav); the single 3D moment is the Apple-style assemble showpiece.`,
  },
]

phase('Build')
log('Building 3 candidate styleframes in parallel (A blueprint / B genesis / C kinetic-index)')
const results = await parallel(CANDIDATES.map(c => () =>
  agent(
    SHARED +
    c.brief +
    `\n\n== EXACT OUTPUT PATHS ==\n- styleframe HTML: ${c.html}\n- candidate spec doc: ${c.spec}\nCreate both files now. The directories may not exist yet — Write will create them.`,
    { label: `build:route-${c.id}`, phase: 'Build' }
  )
))
return results.map((r, i) => ({ route: CANDIDATES[i].id, summary: r }))

input.script:
export const meta = {
  name: 'sprint01-judge-panel',
  description: '3 critic personas score the A/B/C styleframes against the brief rubric',
  phases: [{ title: 'Judge', detail: 'Awwwards juror, brand-fit, perf/feasibility' }],
}

const ROOT = '/Users/prynq/Desktop/kartar/sachmeetsb.github.io'

const AXES = `
Score each route 1-5 (integers) on EACH of these 5 axes:
1. distinctiveness — Does not read as the old site (saffron/indigo SaaS), nor generic dark-SaaS. A stranger would not confuse it with a template.
2. fit — Fit to positioning "builders of autonomous AI for Indian industry": engineered competence + scale; not whimsical, not mystical. The CustomsIQ pipeline and industries feel native to the aesthetic.
3. achievability — Achievable by coding agents with NO 3D artist: showpieces buildable from procedural/shader/type/SVG within draw budgets (the locked WebGL ledger: H2 CustomsIQ graph assemble/disassemble scrub [mandatory], W2 industry skyline instanced extrude [mandatory], H1 hero idle object [downgradable to CSS/SVG]). Penalize dependence on hand-authored 3D, heavy particle sims, or fragile blend/glow stacks.
4. mobile — Mobile degradability: each act has an obvious, non-blank mobile/static form. Penalize aesthetics that ONLY work with WebGL/motion.
5. execution — Execution confidence at autonomous-sprint pace: the styleframe already looks finished, not 60%; few flaky dependencies.
`

const SCHEMA = {
  type: 'object',
  additionalProperties: false,
  required: ['critic', 'scores', 'verdict'],
  properties: {
    critic: { type: 'string' },
    scores: {
      type: 'object', additionalProperties: false, required: ['A', 'B', 'C'],
      properties: Object.fromEntries(['A', 'B', 'C'].map(r => [r, {
        type: 'object', additionalProperties: false,
        required: ['distinctiveness', 'fit', 'achievability', 'mobile', 'execution', 'note'],
        properties: {
          distinctiveness: { type: 'integer', minimum: 1, maximum: 5 },
          fit: { type: 'integer', minimum: 1, maximum: 5 },
          achievability: { type: 'integer', minimum: 1, maximum: 5 },
          mobile: { type: 'integer', minimum: 1, maximum: 5 },
          execution: { type: 'integer', minimum: 1, maximum: 5 },
          note: { type: 'string', description: 'two-sentence justification citing what you SAW in the frames' },
        },
      }])),
    },
    verdict: { type: 'string', description: 'one paragraph: which route you would pick and why' },
  },
}

const SHARED = `
You are one critic on a 3-person judge panel choosing the art direction for the kartar.ai rebuild ("Kartar AI Labs" — builders of autonomous AI for Indian industry; brand feeling = competence that makes things real). Three competing candidate directions were built as Home-route styleframe slices.

READ the visual evidence — open ALL of these image files with the Read tool and study them before scoring:
- ${ROOT}/plans/design/styleframes/route-A.png (above-fold) and route-A-full.png (full slice) — Candidate A "Blueprint→Built" (drafting-table, graphite + bone + blueprint blue)
- ${ROOT}/plans/design/styleframes/route-B.png and route-B-full.png — Candidate B "Genesis / Raw Matter" (particle coalescence, deep space + violet→blue duotone)
- ${ROOT}/plans/design/styleframes/route-C.png and route-C-full.png — Candidate C "Kinetic Index" (light editorial, bone paper + ink + oxblood, type-as-machine)
Also Read the three spec docs:
- ${ROOT}/plans/design/candidates/route-A-blueprint.md
- ${ROOT}/plans/design/candidates/route-B-genesis.md
- ${ROOT}/plans/design/candidates/route-C-kinetic-index.md

Context you must weigh: the site will be BUILT BY AUTONOMOUS CODING AGENTS (no 3D artist, no paid assets) as a 4-route React SPA with GSAP/Lenis + max 3 WebGL showpieces; mobile (<=768) and reduced-motion get static designed fallbacks with ZERO three.js bytes. Two prior overhauls were rejected: one too close to the old saffron/indigo look, one a dark "forge" concept that didn't land. Forbidden as primary: saffron #F2A93B, indigo #2D1B69/#4A2F9A, teal #00BFA5, ember #FF5E0E.
${AXES}
Score pixels first, prose second. Be a harsh, honest expert — use the full 1-5 range; do not cluster everything at 4. Judge each frame on what is actually VISIBLE: hierarchy, contrast, legibility at rendered size, spacing rhythm, whether it looks finished, whether it reads as a template.
`

const CRITICS = [
  { key: 'awwwards-juror', persona: `PERSONA: You are an Awwwards/FWA jury member and art director. You care about distinctiveness, typographic craft, grid discipline, art-directed detail, and whether a frame would survive a Site-of-the-Day jury. You have seen ten thousand dark-gradient AI templates and you are tired of them. You punish genericism, muddy hierarchy, and fake-looking polish; you reward a confident, ownable visual voice.` },
  { key: 'brand-fit', persona: `PERSONA: You are the brand strategist for Kartar AI Labs. Positioning: BUILDERS of autonomous AI for INDIAN industry — customs brokers, factories, logistics, law firms. Buyers are pragmatic operators and founders, not design-twitter. You ask: does this aesthetic say "engineered competence that makes things real"? Does CustomsIQ (HS codes, duty, ₹ figures) feel native here? Would an Indian enterprise CTO trust this company with production systems — or does it feel like a vibe?` },
  { key: 'perf-feasibility', persona: `PERSONA: You are the performance/feasibility engineer who must LIVE with this choice for 16 autonomous sprints. You care about: can coding agents actually build each act at this quality bar; WebGL risk (particle sims, glow stacks, blend modes = flaky + GPU-hungry); mobile/static degrade quality (a light editorial page degrades trivially; a particle universe degrades hard); font/byte budgets; contrast/a11y (axe color-contrast gates every route — judge visible text contrast harshly); and whether the styleframe's beauty depends on motion that a static mobile tier cannot have.` },
]

phase('Judge')
log('3 critics scoring routes A/B/C against the weighted rubric')
const results = await parallel(CRITICS.map(c => () =>
  agent(SHARED + '\n' + c.persona + `\n\nSet critic="${c.key}" in your structured output.`,
    { label: `judge:${c.key}`, phase: 'Judge', schema: SCHEMA })
))
return results.filter(Boolean)

# Candidate A — BLUEPRINT → BUILT

## Mood
Precise, confident, engineered — the quiet luxury of a well-made tool, where ghost wireframe linework inks into built material under a draftsman's hand.

## Palette
| Role | Hex | Use |
|------|-----|-----|
| ground | `#0C0D10` | near-black graphite drafting field (dominant) |
| surface | `#15171C` / `#101216` | inked panels, title blocks, stage cells |
| ink | `#EDE7DA` | warm paper/bone — display + primary body text |
| ink-dim | `#C9C3B6` | secondary body, leader-line labels |
| muted | `#7C7F87` / `#4A4D55` | mono dimension marks, gutters, ticks |
| line | `#23262D` / `#2E323B` | drafting grid, panel borders, dimension rules |
| accent | `#3B7CFF` | the live blueprint line — committed (cyan-leaning blueprint blue) |

Accent (`#3B7CFF`) coverage is held **under 10%** of any frame: it appears only as thin structural strokes, ~5 small node fills, one "Now" inked glyph-fragment, the primary CTA, and the confidence bar. Greys + bone carry all mass. No forbidden colors used (no saffron / indigo / teal / ember).

## Type
- **Display** — Clash Display (Fontshare), weights 600/700; geometric, used for "ALL IS. NOW", section H2s, stage names, industry names.
- **Body** — Satoshi (Fontshare), weights 400/500/700; sublines, descriptions, CTAs.
- **Mono** — JetBrains Mono (Google), weights 400/500/700; dimension marks, title-block stamps, agent.trace spec table, all measured numbers (`tnum`).

## Motion thesis
Ghost wireframes **ink in** and snap to a solid built state on a `cubic-bezier(.7,0,.2,1)` 0.5–0.7s authoritative move. Dominant travel vectors are multi-directional: **(1) horizontal** — the 5-stage pipeline rail draws + fills left→right; **(2) depth** — the hero polyhedron extrudes wireframe→solid toward the viewer (z), and the H2 CustomsIQ panels assemble from screen edges→center; **(3) centripetal** — knowledge-graph satellite nodes pull inward along leader lines to the HS 8471 root; with a **counter-parallax** of the drafting grid drifting opposite the scroll.

## Signature WebGL moments
- **H2 — CustomsIQ assemble/disassemble (mandatory):** the two blueprint panels (graph schematic + agent.trace) fly in as drafted fragments from the four screen edges, leader lines drawing via `stroke-dashoffset`, nodes snapping to grid with slight overshoot, then the trace rows type/ink top→down to `confidence 0.94`. Reverse on scroll-out. *Degrade:* CSS staggered fade+translate of the two panels; reduced-motion → static fully-inked sheet (the still rendered here).
- **W2 — industry-skyline extrude (mandatory):** the 8-industry index cells extrude upward into a drafted isometric "skyline" of blueprint volumes on hover/scroll, each capped with its index number; tallest = currently-deployed. *Degrade:* flat index grid with corner ticks (as shown); reduced-motion → no extrude, accent name highlight only.
- **H1 — hero idle object (may downgrade):** the knowledge-graph polyhedron idles as a slow wireframe⇄solid breathe, top faces inked blue, lower scaffolding dashed-ghost. *Degrade:* inline SVG with a 6s ambient loop whose every frame reads composed; reduced-motion → static half-inked polyhedron (rendered here as SVG).

## Page IA
**LOCKED.** Routes: `/`, `/work`, `/studio`, `/contact`. Home order: H0 preloader → H1 hero → H2 pipeline → H3 industries marquee → H4 services ×3 → H5 testimonials ×3 → H6 CTA.

## Reference anchors
- **obys.agency** — kinetic technical type locked to a visible grid; mono measurement annotations on the display headline.
- **ten.375.studio** — ruler/axis nav language: dimension lines, registration ticks, title-block stamps as wayfinding.
- **apple.com/airpods scrub** — assemble-from-parts choreography for the H2 CustomsIQ panels and the hero polyhedron extrude.
