1	# Candidate B — GENESIS / RAW MATTER
2	
3	## Mood
4	Cosmic-but-controlled emergence — a deep-space void where formless matter gathers, under quiet mastery, into legible product, selling competence as an act of creation rather than mysticism.
5	
6	## Palette
7	| Hex | Role | Notes |
8	|---|---|---|
9	| `#06070C` | ground | deep space charcoal/black, the void |
10	| `#0A0C14` | surface | raised void panels / glass base |
11	| `#0E111C` / `#141826` | surface-2 | dark-glass trace + KG panels |
12	| `#1E2334` / `#2A3146` | line / muted-line | hairlines, rings, dividers |
13	| `#EDEFF7` | ink | cool off-white "matter" (display + headings) |
14	| `#A6ABBF` | muted | body copy |
15	| `#6A7088` | muted-2 | mono labels, dimension marks |
16	| `#7C5CFF → #2FD0FF` | accent | violet→electric-blue energy duotone — the ONLY color |
17	
18	Accent coverage: the violet→electric-blue gradient appears only on the coalescing nucleus front, the KG bright nodes/edges, CTA pills, active-stage rings, the "Now." mark, and a few italic word-fills — well under 10% of either screenshot. Page is otherwise pure monochrome void + cool white. No forbidden colors (saffron/indigo/teal/ember) present — duotone is outside the banned set.
19	
20	## Type
21	- **Display:** Boska (Fontshare) — weights 700 (headlines/h2/stats) and 900 (the "All Is. Now." re-set). Italic 500 for energy-gradient emphasis phrases.
22	- **Body:** Hanken Grotesk (Google) — 400 body / 500–600 nav + buttons / 800 wordmark. Wide, airy.
23	- **Mono:** Space Mono (Google) — 400/700 for eyebrows, dimension marks, agent.trace, stage names, the "AI" mark, indices.
24	
25	## Motion thesis
26	Things GATHER, not slide: scattered point clouds coalesce from screen edges toward composed centers and then idle-orbit. Dominant travel vectors are multi-directional — (1) **centripetal** inward coalescence of the hero nucleus from a 360° scatter ring, (2) **depth** via per-particle z-mapped size/opacity and a radial energy glow front, (3) **horizontal** drift in the industries marquee, plus (4) **counter-parallax** ambient orbital drift on settled core particles and twinkling KG nodes — all easing on expo.out and fully settling by ~1.3s.
27	
28	## Signature WebGL moments
29	- **H2 — CustomsIQ assemble/disassemble [mandatory]:** the knowledge graph rendered as a *constellation being born* — HS-8471 family nodes are gathered bright points (root + hot nodes carry the duotone with a radial bloom), edges are faint violet→blue light lines; on full Home it assembles from screen edges→center with stage names (Read/Ground/Reason/Classify/Cite) lit as waypoints along the coalescence, then disperses on scroll-out. Degrade: prefers-reduced-motion / mobile renders the final settled constellation as a static canvas snapshot (no gather), trace panel as plain dark glass.
30	- **W2 — industry-skyline extrude [mandatory]:** the 8 verticals extrude as point-cloud "towers" rising out of the dot-field horizon, each label condensing from particles at its peak; mobile degrades to the CSS marquee row of glass chips shown here (index + duotone node dot).
31	- **H1 — hero idle object [CSS/SVG-downgradable]:** the particle nucleus — ~520 dots forming a tilted twin-ring shell + dense gathered core (a forming planet/nucleus), coalescing on expo.out then idle-orbiting with faint constellation lines across the core. Implemented here as a canvas point cloud; degrades to a static SVG dot-orb on reduced-motion.
32	
33	## Page IA
34	LOCKED — routes: `/`, `/work`, `/studio`, `/contact`. Home order: **H0** preloader → **H1** hero → **H2** CustomsIQ pipeline → **H3** industries marquee → **H4** services ×3 → **H5** testimonials ×3 → **H6** CTA. This styleframe is the vertical slice H1 (hero) + H2 (CustomsIQ act) + the head of H3 (industries marquee).
35	
36	## Reference anchors
37	- **lusion.co** — progressive reveal pacing and genuine depth; borrowed the z-mapped particle size/opacity and the radial energy glow front for a sense of volume in the void.
38	- **Codrops R3F image-reveal** — matter→image coalescence; borrowed the "scattered dots gather into a recognizable form" mechanic for the hero nucleus and the divider scatter→order gradient.
39	- **storytelling.noomoagency.com** — chaptered emergence; borrowed the indexed `[ 00 ] / [ 01 ]` act-numbering and the staged waypoint progression (Read→Cite) so each scroll act reads as a discrete birth.
40

## Mood
Loud, confident, design-forward editorial studio — Swiss grid broken on purpose, giant kinetic type as the machine itself, light paper ground inverting the dark-SaaS cliché.

## Palette
| Hex | Role | Notes |
|-----|------|-------|
| `#F4F1EA` | ground | bone / paper, dominant field |
| `#ECE8DE` | surface | panel fill (trace + graph cards) |
| `#16140F` | ink | near-black, body + display type, inverted bands |
| `#C8472E` | accent | oxblood — the ONE hot accent |
| `rgba(22,20,15,.45–.70)` | muted | ink at reduced opacity for secondary text / hairlines |

Accent coverage: oxblood appears only as the wordmark AI-mark dot, "Now" serif italic, the `30 years` strike, one marquee word, the `Cite` stage + confidence meter, one graph node/edge, and the cursor — measured well under 10% of either frame. No forbidden colors (#F2A93B / #2D1B69 / #4A2F9A / #00BFA5 / #FF5E0E) used anywhere.

## Type
- **Display:** Clash Display (Fontshare) — weights 600/700, the primary structural face; oversized hero at 150px and bleed word at 300px.
- **Display accent (serif):** Gambetta (Fontshare) — italic 400/500, used as editorial punctuation ("All Is.", "Now", "— and shows its work", index sub-labels).
- **Body:** Satoshi (Fontshare) — 400/500/700, sublines and descriptions.
- **Mono:** JetBrains Mono (Google) — 400/500, metadata, coordinates, indexes, trace numbers, nav links.

## Motion thesis
Type is the moving object: oversized display overshoots and settles while marquees travel on their own horizontal axis. Travel vectors — (1) **horizontal** xPercent marquee coupled to scroll velocity; (2) **depth** counter-parallax of the 300px bleed "NOW" against the foreground headline; (3) **centripetal** CustomsIQ assemble from screen edges inward to center; (4) the single allowed **skew-on-velocity** on the industries band.

## Signature WebGL moments
- **H2 — CustomsIQ assemble/disassemble [mandatory, WebGL]:** the five stage plates (Read/Ground/Reason/Classify/Cite) and the trace specimen rows fly in from the four screen edges and lock to a centered typeset grid, Apple-airpods-scrub style, settling by 1.5s. Degrade: CSS grid that fades/translates in, then static (mobile/reduced-motion).
- **W2 — industry skyline extrude [mandatory, WebGL]:** on /work, the 8 industry words extrude into a typographic skyline along the z-axis as the marquee scrubs. Degrade: the flat skewed marquee row shown in this frame (CSS keyframe loop), static on reduced-motion.
- **H1 — hero idle object [downgraded to CSS/SVG]:** the bleed "NOW" word + magnetic oxblood cursor act as the idle signature; pure CSS/SVG, no WebGL. Degrade: cursor hidden, static word on touch.

## Page IA
Routes (LOCKED): `/` `/work` `/studio` `/contact`.
Home order (LOCKED): H0 preloader → H1 hero → H2 pipeline (CustomsIQ) → H3 industries marquee → H4 services ×3 → H5 testimonials ×3 → H6 CTA.

## Reference anchors
- **obys.agency** — kinetic technical type as primary object; oversized cropped display, mono metadata, index numbering.
- **cuberto.com** — magnetic oxblood cursor with trailing ring.
- **ten.375.studio** — axis-based navigation and the index/table-of-contents rhythm with hairline rules.
- **apple.com/airpods scrub** — the single 3D moment: the CustomsIQ assemble showpiece (edges → center).

# JUDGE — Sprint 01 panel verdict

Three critic personas scored the three candidate styleframes (screenshots in
`plans/design/styleframes/`, specs in `plans/design/candidates/`) against the
DIRECTION_BRIEF §1.4 rubric. Scores are 1–5 per axis. Weights: Distinctiveness
25%, Fit-to-positioning 25%, Achievability-by-coding-agents 25%, Mobile
degradability 15%, Execution confidence 10%. Tie-break rule (C > A > B on risk)
was not needed.

## Critic 1 — Awwwards juror (distinctiveness, craft)

| Route | Distinct. | Fit | Achiev. | Mobile | Exec. | Weighted |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| A Blueprint→Built | 4 | 5 | 5 | 5 | 4 | **4.65** |
| B Genesis | 2 | 3 | 2 | 3 | 3 | 2.50 |
| C Kinetic Index | 5 | 4 | 4 | 4 | 4 | 4.25 |

> Picked A: the drafting-grid system (title-block stamps, dimension ticks, mono
> dim-marks) is an ownable visual language no template ships, and every
> showpiece is SVG/line/type-driven — exactly what autonomous agents can build.
> C is the most distinctive single frame but leans on motion to sing; B's
> particle nucleus is "the most over-used dark-AI template image of the last
> three years" and a fragile ~520-point sim.

## Critic 2 — Brand fit ("builders for Indian industry")

| Route | Distinct. | Fit | Achiev. | Mobile | Exec. | Weighted |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| A Blueprint→Built | 4 | 5 | 5 | 5 | 5 | **4.75** |
| B Genesis | 4 | 2 | 2 | 3 | 4 | 2.85 |
| C Kinetic Index | 5 | 3 | 4 | 4 | 5 | 4.10 |

> Picked A: the only route where CustomsIQ looks *native* — the HS-8471 graph,
> agent.trace spec table and ₹ figures read as an engineered system being
> inspected, which is exactly "competence that makes things real" for a
> pragmatic Indian enterprise CTO. B reads mystical (wrong register for a
> duty-calculation tool); C reads "agency portfolio" more than "production AI
> vendor".

## Critic 3 — Performance / feasibility

| Route | Distinct. | Fit | Achiev. | Mobile | Exec. | Weighted |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| A Blueprint→Built | 4 | 5 | 5 | 5 | 5 | **4.75** |
| B Genesis | 4 | 3 | 2 | 2 | 3 | 2.85 |
| C Kinetic Index | 5 | 4 | 4 | 5 | 4 | 4.40 |

> Picked A: every visible element is already SVG line-art, mono type and flat
> panels; no glow stack, no blend-mode dependency, and the reduced-motion tier
> IS the rendered still — near-perfect degrade. Would veto B: its beauty lives
> entirely in a particle coalescence + duotone bloom (flaky, GPU-hungry,
> low-contrast static fallback).

## Panel composite (mean of critic weighted scores)

| Route | Composite | Rank |
|---|---|---|
| **A — Blueprint→Built** | **4.72** | **1 — WINNER** |
| C — Kinetic Index | 4.25 | 2 |
| B — Genesis | 2.73 | 3 |

Per-axis panel means — A: distinctiveness 4.00, fit 5.00, achievability 5.00,
mobile 5.00, execution 4.67. C: 5.00 / 3.67 / 4.00 / 4.33 / 4.33.
B: 3.33 / 2.67 / 2.00 / 2.67 / 3.33.

## Verdict

**Winner: Route A — "Blueprint → Built".** The pick is unanimous (3/3 critics)
and decisive on the three heaviest axes: it is the only direction where the
CustomsIQ pipeline, HS codes and ₹ figures feel native rather than decorative
(fit 5.0), where every act is buildable by coding agents from SVG strokes, mono
type and flat drafted panels with no particle sims or glow stacks
(achievability 5.0), and where the mobile/reduced-motion tier is literally the
rendered still (mobile 5.0). Its one conceded axis — distinctiveness 4.0 vs
C's 5.0 — is mitigated by the drafting-table vocabulary (title blocks,
dimension marks, registration ticks, half-inked glyphs) that no dark-SaaS
template ships. C "Kinetic Index" is the strong runner-up and we graft its best
ideas where they fit route A's language (velocity-coupled industries band,
index/table-of-contents rhythm); B "Genesis" is rejected as a beautiful but
generic, mystical, high-risk particle direction — the exact profile the brief
warns against.

**Chosen route id: A**

# TOKENS — "Blueprint → Built" (Route A, winner per JUDGE.md)

The machine-leaning token source. S02 implements this verbatim in
`tailwind.config.*` + a `:root` CSS-vars block in the global stylesheet.
CSS custom-property prefix: **`--k-`**. Authority: PROTOCOL > CONTRACTS > this
file > sprint files. Do not invent colors/eases outside this table.

---

## 1. Color tokens

| Tailwind key | CSS var | Hex | Role |
|---|---|---|---|
| `ground` | `--k-ground` | `#0C0D10` | near-black graphite drafting field — the page ground, dominant |
| `surface` | `--k-surface` | `#15171C` | inked panels, title blocks, stage cells |
| `surface-2` | `--k-surface-2` | `#101216` | recessed panel fill (trace tables, graph sheets) |
| `line` | `--k-line` | `#23262D` | drafting grid, panel borders, hairlines |
| `line-2` | `--k-line-2` | `#2E323B` | emphasized rules, dimension lines |
| `ink` | `--k-ink` | `#EDE7DA` | warm paper/bone — display + primary text ("built" material) |
| `ink-dim` | `--k-ink-dim` | `#C9C3B6` | secondary body, leader-line labels |
| `muted` | `--k-muted` | `#7C7F87` | mono dimension marks, metadata, eyebrows |
| `muted-2` | `--k-muted-2` | `#4A4D55` | gutters, ticks, ghost annotations |
| `accent` | `--k-accent` | `#3B7CFF` | the live blueprint line — THE one saturated hue |
| `accent-strong` | `--k-accent-strong` | `#2E63D6` | accent hover/pressed |
| `accent-soft` | `--k-accent-soft` | `rgba(59,124,255,0.14)` | accent washes, focus rings, active-stage fills |

Rules:
- **Accent ≤ 10% of any frame.** `#3B7CFF` appears only as: thin structural
  strokes, node fills, the primary CTA, active pipeline stage, confidence bar,
  one inked glyph-fragment per viewport at most.
- Greys + bone carry all mass. Body text is `ink`/`ink-dim` on `ground`/`surface`
  (contrast ≥ 12:1 / ≥ 9:1 — passes axe AA). Never set body copy in `accent`
  (4.0:1 on ground — large text/strokes only) or `muted-2`.
- Forbidden (old brand — never as any token; listed here only as the forbidden set): `#F2A93B`, `#2D1B69`, `#4A2F9A`, `#00BFA5`, `#FF5E0E`.

## 2. Type families + self-hosted WOFF2 plan

| Slot | Family | Weights used | File (in `public/fonts/`) | Est. size |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Display | **Clash Display** (Fontshare) | 600 semibold | `ClashDisplay-Semibold.woff2` | ~45 KB |
| Display | Clash Display | 700 bold | `ClashDisplay-Bold.woff2` | ~45 KB |
| Body | **Satoshi** (Fontshare) | 400/500/700 via variable | `Satoshi-Variable.woff2` | ~60 KB |
| Mono | **JetBrains Mono** (Google) | 400/500 via variable | `JetBrainsMono-Variable.woff2` | ~60 KB |

- Budget: **4 files, ≤ 220 KB total** (subset to Latin + `₹ → · ✓` glyphs). All
  `@font-face` with `font-display: swap`. NO Google/Fontshare CDN `<link>` in the
  shipped app (the styleframes used CDN as throwaway-only).
- Tailwind: `font-display` → `'Clash Display', system-ui, sans-serif`;
  `font-body` → `'Satoshi', system-ui, sans-serif`;
  `font-mono` → `'JetBrains Mono', ui-monospace, monospace`.
- All text splitting/measuring waits on `document.fonts.ready` (scar #3 timing).
- Mono numerals always `font-variant-numeric: tabular-nums` (`tnum`).

## 3. Type scale

| Token | Value | Usage |
|---|---|---|
| `text-hero` | `clamp(3.25rem, 8.5vw, 7.5rem)` / leading `0.95` / tracking `-0.02em` | H1 display ("All Is. Now"), Clash 700 |
| `text-display` | `clamp(2.25rem, 4.5vw, 4rem)` / leading `1.02` / tracking `-0.015em` | section H2s, Clash 600 |
| `text-title` | `clamp(1.375rem, 2.2vw, 2rem)` / leading `1.15` | card/stage titles, Clash 600 |
| `text-body-lg` | `clamp(1.0625rem, 1.4vw, 1.25rem)` / leading `1.6` | hero sublines, intros, Satoshi 400/500 |
| `text-body` | `1rem` / leading `1.7` | body copy, Satoshi 400 |
| `text-small` | `0.875rem` / leading `1.6` | captions, metadata prose |
| `text-mono-label` | `0.6875rem` / leading `1.4` / tracking `0.12em` / uppercase | dimension marks, eyebrows, title-block stamps, JetBrains 500 |
| `text-mono-trace` | `0.8125rem` / leading `1.8` | agent.trace rows, spec tables, JetBrains 400 |

## 4. Spacing, layout, radii, line-work

| Token | Value | Usage |
|---|---|---|
| `--k-section-pad` | `clamp(6rem, 12vh, 10rem)` | vertical section rhythm |
| `--k-container` | `max-width: 1280px`, gutter `clamp(1.25rem, 4vw, 3rem)` | main column |
| `--k-grid-cell` | `48px` | the visible drafting-grid pitch (background grid) |
| `radius-sm` | `2px` | buttons, chips — drafted, near-sharp |
| `radius-md` | `6px` | panels, cards |
| `radius-lg` | `10px` | large sheets only |
| stroke widths | `1px` grid/hairline · `1.5px` drafted line · `2px` accent live line | SVG + borders |

## 5. Motion constants (`src/lib/motion.js` — S03/S04 implement)

| Constant | Value | Usage |
|---|---|---|
| `EASE.PRIMARY` | `cubic-bezier(0.7, 0, 0.2, 1)` — register as GSAP `CustomEase` `"kPrimary"` | every entrance/strike/ink-in; the house move |
| `EASE.SETTLE` | `"expo.out"` | cooling/settling after a strike; scrub latches |
| `EASE.SNAP` | `"back.out(1.4)"` | grid-lock overshoot ONLY (cards/nodes snapping); never on opacity |
| `DUR.XS` | `0.3s` | micro state changes (the law-14 floor — nothing under 0.3s) |
| `DUR.SM` | `0.5s` | small element strikes |
| `DUR.MD` | `0.6s` | the default authoritative move |
| `DUR.LG` | `0.7s` | full-section strikes (the ceiling for entrances) |
| `DUR.SCENE` | `1.0s` | pinned-scene settles / latch commits |
| `STAGGER.LINE` | `0.06s` | line-by-line text |
| `STAGGER.CELL` | `0.04s` | grid cells, trace rows, chars |
| wipe total | `≤ 700ms` (in+out) | route chapter-wipe (locked mechanism) |
| reduced-motion wipe | `≤ 200ms` cross-fade | per locked degrade matrix |

No ad-hoc beziers in section files — import from `src/lib/motion.js` only
(motion law 1). Personality: mechanical precision; weighty, deliberate, never
bouncy; linework draws via `stroke-dashoffset`; elements snap to grid with
slight overshoot then LOCK.

## 6. Tailwind wiring sketch (S02)

```js
// tailwind.config.* (S02 implements; values from §1–§4 above)
theme: {
  extend: {
    colors: { ground:'var(--k-ground)', surface:'var(--k-surface)', 'surface-2':'var(--k-surface-2)',
      line:'var(--k-line)', 'line-2':'var(--k-line-2)', ink:'var(--k-ink)', 'ink-dim':'var(--k-ink-dim)',
      muted:'var(--k-muted)', 'muted-2':'var(--k-muted-2)', accent:'var(--k-accent)',
      'accent-strong':'var(--k-accent-strong)' },
    fontFamily: { display:['Clash Display','system-ui','sans-serif'],
      body:['Satoshi','system-ui','sans-serif'], mono:['JetBrains Mono','ui-monospace','monospace'] },
  }
}
```

| `surface-2` | `--k-surface-2` | `#101216` | recessed panel fill (trace tables, graph sheets) |
| `line` | `--k-line` | `#23262D` | drafting grid, panel borders, hairlines |
| `line-2` | `--k-line-2` | `#2E323B` | emphasized rules, dimension lines |
| `ink` | `--k-ink` | `#EDE7DA` | warm paper/bone — display + primary text ("built" material) |
| `ink-dim` | `--k-ink-dim` | `#C9C3B6` | secondary body, leader-line labels |
| `muted` | `--k-muted` | `#7C7F87` | mono dimension marks, metadata, eyebrows |
| `muted-2` | `--k-muted-2` | `#4A4D55` | gutters, ticks, ghost annotations |
| `accent` | `--k-accent` | `#3B7CFF` | the live blueprint line — THE one saturated hue |
| `accent-strong` | `--k-accent-strong` | `#2E63D6` | accent hover/pressed |
| `accent-soft` | `--k-accent-soft` | `rgba(59,124,255,0.14)` | accent washes, focus rings, active-stage fills |

forever; only H2/W1/W2 are explicitly reversible.

vector that is NOT plain vertical; ≥ 3 acts per route move horizontally or in
depth. Route A's vectors: horizontal (pipeline rail L→R, marquee, filmstrips,
case-study chapters), depth (polyhedron wireframe→solid extrude toward viewer,
CTA type scaling), centripetal (graph nodes pulling inward to HS 8471.30),
counter-parallax (the drafting grid drifts opposite scroll, bg vs fg at
different rates). Graft from C: the industries band may use the single allowed
skew-on-velocity; index/table-of-contents rhythm (numbered rows, hairline
rules) is part of the vocabulary.

**Primitive table** (S04 builds these in `src/components/motion/*`; page
sprints compose ONLY these):

| Primitive | What it does | Reduced/mobile fallback | Scar gate |
|---|---|---|---|

| `SplitLines` | headline lands line-by-line (waits `document.fonts.ready`) | static text | #3, #7 |
| `SnapStack` | cards arrive from right, stack with slight rotation, snap-lock | plain stacked cards, fade-up | #4, #5, #8 |
| `AxisRail` | pinned scrub: marker travels a rail, panels advance laterally | native vertical stack, no pin | #1, #2, #5, #9 |
| `KineticMarquee` | xPercent marquee coupled to shared `scrollBus.velocity` | slow CSS marquee / static list | #8, #10 |
| `MagneticButton` | cursor-magnet CTA (heavier retune) | disabled on touch; normal tap target | #7 |
| `CountStrike` | numbers count up when centered, then hold | final number rendered immediately | #5, #8 |
| `DimensionMark` | mono annotation + leader line fades/draws in | static annotation | #8 |

**The 14 motion laws** (brief §5.1 — LOCKED, condensed): (1) one easing
language from `src/lib/motion.js` only; (2) scrub over trigger; trigger-once
strikes hold forever; (3) high-water latch — reveal progress only grows;
(4) one shared `scrollBus.velocity` writer; (5) no standalone rAF — everything
off `gsap.ticker`/`useFrame`; (6) zero per-frame allocations; (7) no per-frame

window; (9) never `self.kill()` inside `onUpdate` — defer via

unmount+dispose on route-leave, `frameloop="demand"`, zero per-frame
allocations.

horizontal scroll, no 3D canvases, zero `three` bytes, zero horizontal overflow

baseline; motion is additive.

bundle lean). S10/S11/S12 read these briefs:

   hand-rolled 480vh black box). As you scroll, drafted fragments — invoice/doc

3. **Locked IA:** exactly `/`, `/work`, `/studio`, `/contact`; the §IA act

   cover; reduced = cross-fade ≤ 200ms). Do not invent per-route transitions.
6. **Type fences:** Clash Display / Satoshi / JetBrains Mono only — no new
   families, no serif accent (that was Route C), no CDN fonts, 4 WOFF2 files
   ≤ 220 KB.
7. **Motion fences:** the 14 laws + 10 scars are protocol, not suggestions.
   One easing language (`EASE.PRIMARY`/`SETTLE`/`SNAP` from `src/lib/motion.js`).
   No skew except the single velocity-skew on the industries band. Nothing
   bouncy/elastic on opacity. No act that is "fade up in place".
8. **Facts fences:** PROTOCOL §8 — all names/numbers/links/integration ids
   byte-exact, content imported from `src/data/*` only.
9. **Budgets:** PROTOCOL §6 byte/draw/font budgets; mobile ≤ 768 = no pins, no
   horizontal scroll, no 3D, no overflow at 390px; reduced-motion = designed
   static finals, zero `three` bytes.
10. **Vocabulary guard:** the drafting-table language (grid field, dimension
    marks, leader lines, title-block stamps, registration ticks, half-inked
    glyphs, mono spec tables) is THE identity — new sections must speak it, not
    generic dark-SaaS (no glassmorphism, no glow orbs, no particle fields —
    that was Route B, rejected).

| Work | `/work` | Proof | 2 case studies (horizontal chapters) · **W2 9-industry skyline showpiece** · recap CTA |
| Studio | `/studio` | Who builds it | 4-step process (axis rail) · 18-member team grid · manifesto |
| Contact | `/contact` | Convert | EmailJS form (locked ids) · Calendly · `hello@kartar.ai` |

**Per-route act order** (locked; travel vectors per §Motion Language):

- **Home:** H0 preloader ("All Is. Now" strokes draw + 0→100 counter, once per
  session) → H1 hero (headline lands by line; SVG polyhedron idles; bg drafting
  grid counter-parallaxes diagonally) → H2 ★ CustomsIQ pipeline (pinned
  assemble/disassemble) → H3 industries marquee (8, horizontal,
  velocity-coupled) → H4 services (3 cards arrive from right, stack + lock) →
  H5 testimonials (3, horizontal filmstrip) → H6 CTA banner (type scales in
  depth; magnetic Calendly button).
- **Work:** W1 case studies (2, horizontal pinned chapters traveling left;
  metrics count-strike on center) → W2 ★ industry-solutions skyline (9 volumes
  rise under pin while field pans) → W3 recap + magnetic CTA → Contact.
- **Studio:** S1 process axis-rail (marker travels a diagonal/horizontal ruler;
  4 steps pin and engrave) → S2 team grid (18; diagonal stagger;
  alternating-column parallax; initials-fallback avatars; LinkedIn hover) → S3
  manifesto kinetic type.
- **Contact:** C1 split — form panel slides from left, CTA panel from right.
  EmailJS ids/fields/success text byte-exact per CONTRACTS.

**Globals:** Navbar (Home/Work/Studio/Contact from `src/data/nav.js`) + footer
(Sender form `axkAXn` at `#newsletter`, LinkedIn company, Calendly from
`src/data/links.js`). All content imports from `src/data/*` — the facts
contract made executable; no hand-typed content strings in components.
**Page transition (locked mechanism):** horizontal **chapter-wipe** — a
full-bleed *blueprint-paper sheet* (bone `#EDE7DA` with faint grid + title-block
stamp) sweeps across, covers, swaps content at cover-peak, reveals out; total
≤ 700ms; scroll resets to top under cover; reduced-motion = instant cross-fade
≤ 200ms. Implemented as overlay only — never a scroll container (scar #2).

## Motion Language

**Thesis: the page is not scrolling — things are being drafted, inked and
built.** Ghost wireframes ink in and snap to a solid built state on
`EASE.PRIMARY`, 0.5–0.7s, mechanical precision, slight overshoot then LOCK.
Never bouncy, never floaty. "Forged-stays-forged": strikes hold their end state
forever; only H2/W1/W2 are explicitly reversible.

**Multi-directional doctrine (locked):** every act declares a dominant travel
vector that is NOT plain vertical; ≥ 3 acts per route move horizontally or in
depth. Route A's vectors: horizontal (pipeline rail L→R, marquee, filmstrips,
case-study chapters), depth (polyhedron wireframe→solid extrude toward viewer,
CTA type scaling), centripetal (graph nodes pulling inward to HS 8471.30),
counter-parallax (the drafting grid drifts opposite scroll, bg vs fg at
different rates). Graft from C: the industries band may use the single allowed
skew-on-velocity; index/table-of-contents rhythm (numbered rows, hairline
rules) is part of the vocabulary.

**Primitive table** (S04 builds these in `src/components/motion/*`; page
sprints compose ONLY these):

| Primitive | What it does | Reduced/mobile fallback | Scar gate |
|---|---|---|---|
| `InkReveal` | element strikes ghost→inked (opacity/clip + 40px travel), holds forever | static final state | #7, #8 |
| `DraftLine` | SVG stroke-dashoffset line/edge draw | fully-drawn static | #8 |
| `SplitLines` | headline lands line-by-line (waits `document.fonts.ready`) | static text | #3, #7 |
| `SnapStack` | cards arrive from right, stack with slight rotation, snap-lock | plain stacked cards, fade-up | #4, #5, #8 |
| `AxisRail` | pinned scrub: marker travels a rail, panels advance laterally | native vertical stack, no pin | #1, #2, #5, #9 |
| `KineticMarquee` | xPercent marquee coupled to shared `scrollBus.velocity` | slow CSS marquee / static list | #8, #10 |
| `MagneticButton` | cursor-magnet CTA (heavier retune) | disabled on touch; normal tap target | #7 |
| `CountStrike` | numbers count up when centered, then hold | final number rendered immediately | #5, #8 |
| `DimensionMark` | mono annotation + leader line fades/draws in | static annotation | #8 |

**The 14 motion laws** (brief §5.1 — LOCKED, condensed): (1) one easing
language from `src/lib/motion.js` only; (2) scrub over trigger; trigger-once
strikes hold forever; (3) high-water latch — reveal progress only grows;
(4) one shared `scrollBus.velocity` writer; (5) no standalone rAF — everything
off `gsap.ticker`/`useFrame`; (6) zero per-frame allocations; (7) no per-frame
`getBoundingClientRect` — cache on refresh/resize; (8) Lenis scrolls the REAL
window; (9) never `self.kill()` inside `onUpdate` — defer via
`gsap.delayedCall`; (10) ONE coordinated `ScrollTrigger.refresh()` after fonts
+ preloader + lazy settle; (11) `position: relative` on any section hosting an
`absolute inset-0` veil; (12) prefer utilities — injected `<style>` outranks
Tailwind, scope it; (13) StrictMode-idempotent effects, `gsap.context()` +
revert; (14) nothing under 0.3s; minimum perceptible move 40px.

**The 10 scars are protocol** (PROTOCOL §2 — restated so every sprint
inherits): #1 defer ScrollTrigger kills out of `onUpdate`; #2 real-window
scroll, transitions are overlays not scroll containers; #3 one debounced
refresh after fonts/preloader/lazy settle; #4 `relative` under `inset-0`
overlays; #5 `onLeave`/`onLeaveBack` latches so flings commit end states;
#6 scope injected CSS; #7 idempotent effects + GL disposal under StrictMode;
#8 `gsap.matchMedia`, mobile ≤ 768 and `prefers-reduced-motion` gates never
regress, designed static states never blanks; #9 per-route `gsap.context`,
kill old route triggers BEFORE new mount, refresh after transition-in,
`lenis.scrollTo(0, { immediate: true })`; #10 section-local canvases
unmount+dispose on route-leave, `frameloop="demand"`, zero per-frame
allocations.

**Tiers (locked degrade matrix):** `full / mid / lite / none` resolved via
`src/lib/useReducedMotion.js` (breakpoint 768). Mobile/lite: no pins, no
horizontal scroll, no 3D canvases, zero `three` bytes, zero horizontal overflow
at 390px. Reduced/none: designed static final states. The static design IS the
baseline; motion is additive.

## Showpieces

Exactly **two WebGL showpieces ship** (H2 + W2). H1 is **decided: downgraded to
CSS/SVG** (brief allows; removes the third GL surface and keeps Home's initial
bundle lean). S10/S11/S12 read these briefs:

1. **H2 ★ — CustomsIQ assemble/disassemble** (Home, mount
   `[data-showpiece="pipeline"]`). Pinned GSAP-scrubbed scene (replaces the old
   hand-rolled 480vh black box). As you scroll, drafted fragments — invoice/doc
   nodes — **fly in from the four screen edges and assemble centripetally** into
   the knowledge graph stage-by-stage: Read → Ground (Ch.84/85/rulings) →
   Reason (GIR, AIDC) → Classify (**HS 8471.30** crystallizes at center) → Cite
   (Notif. node + citation lines draw). Scroll back = disassembles in reverse.
   Material language: nodes are drafted circles (instanced meshes), edges are
   `Line2`/`LineSegments` drawing like leader lines (`stroke-dashoffset` feel),
   labels drei `Html`; the agent.trace panel types/inks rows to
   `confidence 0.94`. Node/edge data from `src/data/pipeline.js`. Latch on
   `onLeave`/`onLeaveBack` (scar #5); draw calls ≤ 40. **Degrade:** lite = 2D
   SVG/Canvas version of the same graph, scrubbed (the styleframe's blueprint
   sheet is the design); none = static finished graph, all nodes lit, HS code
   centered.
2. **W2 ★ — Industry-solutions skyline extrude** (Work, mount
   `[data-showpiece="skyline"]`). The **9 industry agents** as a drafted field
   of blueprint volumes — ONE instanced draw (per-instance extrude + color via
   instanceMatrix/instanceColor, never 9 meshes). Under pin, volumes **rise/
   extrude (depth) while the field pans horizontally**; front-row before→after
   flips and stats count-strike. Technique decided: **instanced-extrude** (not
   image-reveal shader — that was the C path). **Degrade:** lite = DOM/CSS card
   grid with GSAP reveal, no canvas; none = static grid, before/after both
   shown, final numbers.
3. **H1 — hero idle object (DECIDED: CSS/SVG, no WebGL).** The knowledge-graph
   polyhedron as inline SVG: top faces inked blueprint-blue, lower scaffolding
   dashed-ghost, idling on a slow 6s wireframe⇄solid "breathe" whose every
   frame reads composed (mount `[data-showpiece="hero"]` still exists as the
   poster/SVG slot). **Degrade:** none = static half-inked polyhedron (the
   styleframe still).

GL rules (scar #10 + PROTOCOL §6): all `three`/R3F/drei code in lazy chunks
≤ 320 KB gz shared; zero `three` bytes on `/studio`, `/contact`, mobile and
reduced-motion; canvases dispose on route-leave; `frameloop="demand"` where
possible.

## Anti-Wander Rules

The fences. Later sprints may not cross these; violations are gate failures.

1. **Forbidden palette (never as primary/background/token):** saffron
   `#F2A93B`, indigo `#2D1B69` / `#4A2F9A`, teal `#00BFA5`, ember `#FF5E0E`.
   The page must never read as "the old site recolored".
2. **One saturated accent** — `#3B7CFF` only, ≤ 10% of any frame. No second
   hue, no gradients-as-color (the bone/graphite materials carry the design).
   No new colors outside TOKENS.md §1.
3. **Locked IA:** exactly `/`, `/work`, `/studio`, `/contact`; the §IA act
   orders; nothing dropped, no new routes. `#newsletter` lives in the footer
   and must resolve from every route the CTA appears on.
4. **Locked showpiece ledger:** exactly the 3 briefs above — H2 + W2 WebGL,
   H1 CSS/SVG (decided). No additional canvases, no new GL moments.
5. **Locked transition:** the horizontal blueprint-paper chapter-wipe ≤ 700ms
   (AnimatePresence + one GSAP timeline, overlay only, scroll-reset under
   cover; reduced = cross-fade ≤ 200ms). Do not invent per-route transitions.
6. **Type fences:** Clash Display / Satoshi / JetBrains Mono only — no new
   families, no serif accent (that was Route C), no CDN fonts, 4 WOFF2 files
   ≤ 220 KB.
7. **Motion fences:** the 14 laws + 10 scars are protocol, not suggestions.
   One easing language (`EASE.PRIMARY`/`SETTLE`/`SNAP` from `src/lib/motion.js`).
   No skew except the single velocity-skew on the industries band. Nothing
   bouncy/elastic on opacity. No act that is "fade up in place".
8. **Facts fences:** PROTOCOL §8 — all names/numbers/links/integration ids
   byte-exact, content imported from `src/data/*` only.
9. **Budgets:** PROTOCOL §6 byte/draw/font budgets; mobile ≤ 768 = no pins, no
   horizontal scroll, no 3D, no overflow at 390px; reduced-motion = designed
   static finals, zero `three` bytes.
10. **Vocabulary guard:** the drafting-table language (grid field, dimension
    marks, leader lines, title-block stamps, registration ticks, half-inked
    glyphs, mono spec tables) is THE identity — new sections must speak it, not
    generic dark-SaaS (no glassmorphism, no glow orbs, no particle fields —
    that was Route B, rejected).

# CONTRACTS — the living technical contract

The binding interface between sprints. It grows: most sections below are
skeletons now; the named sprint **appends** to them at runtime. When you add a
selector, id, data-attr, global, or API, you MUST record it here in the same
commit, so QA assertions and later sprints stay in sync.

**Authority:** `PROTOCOL > CONTRACTS > DIRECTION/TOKENS > sprint file`. This doc
beats DIRECTION and the sprint file on any **locked id / selector / integration
id**. It does not override PROTOCOL.

**How to append:** find your sprint's section, replace its `(empty — …)`
placeholder or add rows beneath the existing ones. Keep the
`<!-- appended by sprint NN -->` marker so the next author knows where their
turn begins. Never delete a prior sprint's entries; if you supersede one, strike
it and add the replacement with a note.

# DIRECTION — "Blueprint → Built" (the binding art direction)

**Winner: Route A — Blueprint → Built** (JUDGE.md, unanimous 3/3, composite
4.72 vs C 4.25 / B 2.73). The site is a **drafting table where blueprints
resolve into built reality**: ghost wireframe linework, dimension marks and
annotations exist first; as you move, they **ink in**, fill with material, and
snap into finished objects. The feeling sold is *competence that makes things
real* — technical drawing meets product photography, the quiet luxury of a
well-made tool. The proof-of-look is `plans/design/styleframes/route-A.html`
(+ `route-A.png` / `route-A-full.png`); graft from runner-up C only where noted
in §Motion Language.

Authority: `PROTOCOL > CONTRACTS > this doc + TOKENS.md > sprint files`.

---

## Tokens

Full machine source: **`plans/design/TOKENS.md`** (S02 implements it verbatim;
CSS var prefix `--k-`). Summary:

- **Palette** — ground `#0C0D10` graphite · surfaces `#15171C`/`#101216` ·
  lines `#23262D`/`#2E323B` · ink `#EDE7DA` warm bone (+ dim `#C9C3B6`) ·
  muted `#7C7F87`/`#4A4D55` · **one accent `#3B7CFF` blueprint blue**
  (+ strong `#2E63D6`, soft `rgba(59,124,255,0.14)`). Accent ≤ 10% of any
  frame; greys + bone carry all mass; body copy never in accent or `muted-2`.
- **Type** — Display **Clash Display** 600/700; Body **Satoshi** 400/500/700;
  Mono **JetBrains Mono** 400/500 (dimension marks, stamps, traces, all
  numbers `tnum`). Self-hosted WOFF2, 4 files ≤ 220 KB, `font-display: swap`,
  no CDN fonts in the shipped app.
- **Type scale** — hero `clamp(3.25rem, 8.5vw, 7.5rem)`; display
  `clamp(2.25rem, 4.5vw, 4rem)`; body-lg `clamp(1.0625rem, 1.4vw, 1.25rem)`;
  body `1rem/1.7`; mono-label `0.6875rem`, tracking `0.12em`, uppercase.
- **Motion constants** — `EASE.PRIMARY = cubic-bezier(0.7, 0, 0.2, 1)`
  (CustomEase `"kPrimary"`), `EASE.SETTLE = "expo.out"`,
  `EASE.SNAP = "back.out(1.4)"` (grid-lock only); durations 0.3/0.5/0.6/0.7s,
  scene-settle 1.0s; route wipe ≤ 700ms; reduced-motion cross-fade ≤ 200ms.
- **Layout** — container 1280px; section pad `clamp(6rem, 12vh, 10rem)`;
  visible drafting grid pitch 48px; radii 2/6/10px (drafted, near-sharp).

## IA

**LOCKED 4-route map** (brief §2; S02 implements with `react-router-dom`,
`BrowserRouter` + GitHub Pages SPA fallback: `public/404.html` sessionStorage
redirect trick + restore snippet in `index.html`; keep `CNAME` at root AND
`public/CNAME`):

| Route | Path | Purpose | Content (all facts survive — see PROTOCOL §8 / facts.json) |
|---|---|---|---|
| Home | `/` | The cinematic pitch | Hero ("All Is. Now", dual CTA Calendly + See our work) · **H2 CustomsIQ pipeline showpiece** · 8-industries marquee · 3 services · 3 testimonials · CTA banner · footer |
| Work | `/work` | Proof | 2 case studies (horizontal chapters) · **W2 9-industry skyline showpiece** · recap CTA |
| Studio | `/studio` | Who builds it | 4-step process (axis rail) · 18-member team grid · manifesto |
| Contact | `/contact` | Convert | EmailJS form (locked ids) · Calendly · `hello@kartar.ai` |

**Per-route act order** (locked; travel vectors per §Motion Language):

- **Home:** H0 preloader ("All Is. Now" strokes draw + 0→100 counter, once per
  session) → H1 hero (headline lands by line; SVG polyhedron idles; bg drafting
  grid counter-parallaxes diagonally) → H2 ★ CustomsIQ pipeline (pinned

  velocity-coupled) → H4 services (3 cards arrive from right, stack + lock) →

- **Work:** W1 case studies (2, horizontal pinned chapters traveling left;
  metrics count-strike on center) → W2 ★ industry-solutions skyline (9 volumes
  rise under pin while field pans) → W3 recap + magnetic CTA → Contact.
- **Studio:** S1 process axis-rail (marker travels a diagonal/horizontal ruler;
  4 steps pin and engrave) → S2 team grid (18; diagonal stagger;
  alternating-column parallax; initials-fallback avatars; LinkedIn hover) → S3
  manifesto kinetic type.
- **Contact:** C1 split — form panel slides from left, CTA panel from right.

**Globals:** Navbar (Home/Work/Studio/Contact from `src/data/nav.js`) + footer
(Sender form `axkAXn` at `#newsletter`, LinkedIn company, Calendly from
`src/data/links.js`). All content imports from `src/data/*` — the facts
contract made executable; no hand-typed content strings in components.
**Page transition (locked mechanism):** horizontal **chapter-wipe** — a
full-bleed *blueprint-paper sheet* (bone `#EDE7DA` with faint grid + title-block
stamp) sweeps across, covers, swaps content at cover-peak, reveals out; total
≤ 700ms; scroll resets to top under cover; reduced-motion = instant cross-fade
≤ 200ms. Implemented as overlay only — never a scroll container (scar #2).

## Motion Language

**Thesis: the page is not scrolling — things are being drafted, inked and
built.** Ghost wireframes ink in and snap to a solid built state on
`EASE.PRIMARY`, 0.5–0.7s, mechanical precision, slight overshoot then LOCK.
Never bouncy, never floaty. "Forged-stays-forged": strikes hold their end state
forever; only H2/W1/W2 are explicitly reversible.

**Multi-directional doctrine (locked):** every act declares a dominant travel
vector that is NOT plain vertical; ≥ 3 acts per route move horizontally or in
depth. Route A's vectors: horizontal (pipeline rail L→R, marquee, filmstrips,
case-study chapters), depth (polyhedron wireframe→solid extrude toward viewer,
CTA type scaling), centripetal (graph nodes pulling inward to HS 8471.30),
counter-parallax (the drafting grid drifts opposite scroll, bg vs fg at
different rates). Graft from C: the industries band may use the single allowed
skew-on-velocity; index/table-of-contents rhythm (numbered rows, hairline
rules) is part of the vocabulary.

**Primitive table** (S04 builds these in `src/components/motion/*`; page
sprints compose ONLY these):

| Primitive | What it does | Reduced/mobile fallback | Scar gate |
|---|---|---|---|
| `InkReveal` | element strikes ghost→inked (opacity/clip + 40px travel), holds forever | static final state | #7, #8 |
| `DraftLine` | SVG stroke-dashoffset line/edge draw | fully-drawn static | #8 |
| `SplitLines` | headline lands line-by-line (waits `document.fonts.ready`) | static text | #3, #7 |
| `SnapStack` | cards arrive from right, stack with slight rotation, snap-lock | plain stacked cards, fade-up | #4, #5, #8 |
| `AxisRail` | pinned scrub: marker travels a rail, panels advance laterally | native vertical stack, no pin | #1, #2, #5, #9 |
| `KineticMarquee` | xPercent marquee coupled to shared `scrollBus.velocity` | slow CSS marquee / static list | #8, #10 |
| `MagneticButton` | cursor-magnet CTA (heavier retune) | disabled on touch; normal tap target | #7 |
| `CountStrike` | numbers count up when centered, then hold | final number rendered immediately | #5, #8 |
| `DimensionMark` | mono annotation + leader line fades/draws in | static annotation | #8 |

**The 14 motion laws** (brief §5.1 — LOCKED, condensed): (1) one easing
language from `src/lib/motion.js` only; (2) scrub over trigger; trigger-once
strikes hold forever; (3) high-water latch — reveal progress only grows;
(4) one shared `scrollBus.velocity` writer; (5) no standalone rAF — everything
off `gsap.ticker`/`useFrame`; (6) zero per-frame allocations; (7) no per-frame
`getBoundingClientRect` — cache on refresh/resize; (8) Lenis scrolls the REAL
window; (9) never `self.kill()` inside `onUpdate` — defer via
`gsap.delayedCall`; (10) ONE coordinated `ScrollTrigger.refresh()` after fonts
+ preloader + lazy settle; (11) `position: relative` on any section hosting an
`absolute inset-0` veil; (12) prefer utilities — injected `<style>` outranks
Tailwind, scope it; (13) StrictMode-idempotent effects, `gsap.context()` +
revert; (14) nothing under 0.3s; minimum perceptible move 40px.

**The 10 scars are protocol** (PROTOCOL §2 — restated so every sprint
inherits): #1 defer ScrollTrigger kills out of `onUpdate`; #2 real-window
scroll, transitions are overlays not scroll containers; #3 one debounced
refresh after fonts/preloader/lazy settle; #4 `relative` under `inset-0`
overlays; #5 `onLeave`/`onLeaveBack` latches so flings commit end states;
#6 scope injected CSS; #7 idempotent effects + GL disposal under StrictMode;
#8 `gsap.matchMedia`, mobile ≤ 768 and `prefers-reduced-motion` gates never
regress, designed static states never blanks; #9 per-route `gsap.context`,
kill old route triggers BEFORE new mount, refresh after transition-in,
`lenis.scrollTo(0, { immediate: true })`; #10 section-local canvases
unmount+dispose on route-leave, `frameloop="demand"`, zero per-frame
allocations.

**Tiers (locked degrade matrix):** `full / mid / lite / none` resolved via
`src/lib/useReducedMotion.js` (breakpoint 768). Mobile/lite: no pins, no
horizontal scroll, no 3D canvases, zero `three` bytes, zero horizontal overflow
at 390px. Reduced/none: designed static final states. The static design IS the
baseline; motion is additive.

## Showpieces

Exactly **two WebGL showpieces ship** (H2 + W2). H1 is **decided: downgraded to
CSS/SVG** (brief allows; removes the third GL surface and keeps Home's initial
bundle lean). S10/S11/S12 read these briefs:

1. **H2 ★ — CustomsIQ assemble/disassemble** (Home, mount
   `[data-showpiece="pipeline"]`). Pinned GSAP-scrubbed scene (replaces the old
   hand-rolled 480vh black box). As you scroll, drafted fragments — invoice/doc
   nodes — **fly in from the four screen edges and assemble centripetally** into
   the knowledge graph stage-by-stage: Read → Ground (Ch.84/85/rulings) →
   Reason (GIR, AIDC) → Classify (**HS 8471.30** crystallizes at center) → Cite
   (Notif. node + citation lines draw). Scroll back = disassembles in reverse.
   Material language: nodes are drafted circles (instanced meshes), edges are
   `Line2`/`LineSegments` drawing like leader lines (`stroke-dashoffset` feel),
   labels drei `Html`; the agent.trace panel types/inks rows to
   `confidence 0.94`. Node/edge data from `src/data/pipeline.js`. Latch on
   `onLeave`/`onLeaveBack` (scar #5); draw calls ≤ 40. **Degrade:** lite = 2D
   SVG/Canvas version of the same graph, scrubbed (the styleframe's blueprint
   sheet is the design); none = static finished graph, all nodes lit, HS code
   centered.
2. **W2 ★ — Industry-solutions skyline extrude** (Work, mount
   `[data-showpiece="skyline"]`). The **9 industry agents** as a drafted field
   of blueprint volumes — ONE instanced draw (per-instance extrude + color via
   instanceMatrix/instanceColor, never 9 meshes). Under pin, volumes **rise/
   extrude (depth) while the field pans horizontally**; front-row before→after
   flips and stats count-strike. Technique decided: **instanced-extrude** (not
   image-reveal shader — that was the C path). **Degrade:** lite = DOM/CSS card
   grid with GSAP reveal, no canvas; none = static grid, before/after both
   shown, final numbers.
3. **H1 — hero idle object (DECIDED: CSS/SVG, no WebGL).** The knowledge-graph
   polyhedron as inline SVG: top faces inked blueprint-blue, lower scaffolding
   dashed-ghost, idling on a slow 6s wireframe⇄solid "breathe" whose every
   frame reads composed (mount `[data-showpiece="hero"]` still exists as the
   poster/SVG slot). **Degrade:** none = static half-inked polyhedron (the
   styleframe still).

GL rules (scar #10 + PROTOCOL §6): all `three`/R3F/drei code in lazy chunks
≤ 320 KB gz shared; zero `three` bytes on `/studio`, `/contact`, mobile and
reduced-motion; canvases dispose on route-leave; `frameloop="demand"` where
possible.

## Anti-Wander Rules

The fences. Later sprints may not cross these; violations are gate failures.

1. **Forbidden palette (never as primary/background/token):** saffron
   `#F2A93B`, indigo `#2D1B69` / `#4A2F9A`, teal `#00BFA5`, ember `#FF5E0E`.
   The page must never read as "the old site recolored".
2. **One saturated accent** — `#3B7CFF` only, ≤ 10% of any frame. No second
   hue, no gradients-as-color (the bone/graphite materials carry the design).
   No new colors outside TOKENS.md §1.
3. **Locked IA:** exactly `/`, `/work`, `/studio`, `/contact`; the §IA act
   orders; nothing dropped, no new routes. `#newsletter` lives in the footer
   and must resolve from every route the CTA appears on.
4. **Locked showpiece ledger:** exactly the 3 briefs above — H2 + W2 WebGL,
   H1 CSS/SVG (decided). No additional canvases, no new GL moments.
5. **Locked transition:** the horizontal blueprint-paper chapter-wipe ≤ 700ms
   (AnimatePresence + one GSAP timeline, overlay only, scroll-reset under
   cover; reduced = cross-fade ≤ 200ms). Do not invent per-route transitions.
6. **Type fences:** Clash Display / Satoshi / JetBrains Mono only — no new
   families, no serif accent (that was Route C), no CDN fonts, 4 WOFF2 files
   ≤ 220 KB.
7. **Motion fences:** the 14 laws + 10 scars are protocol, not suggestions.
   One easing language (`EASE.PRIMARY`/`SETTLE`/`SNAP` from `src/lib/motion.js`).
   No skew except the single velocity-skew on the industries band. Nothing
   bouncy/elastic on opacity. No act that is "fade up in place".
8. **Facts fences:** PROTOCOL §8 — all names/numbers/links/integration ids
   byte-exact, content imported from `src/data/*` only.
9. **Budgets:** PROTOCOL §6 byte/draw/font budgets; mobile ≤ 768 = no pins, no
   horizontal scroll, no 3D, no overflow at 390px; reduced-motion = designed
   static finals, zero `three` bytes.
10. **Vocabulary guard:** the drafting-table language (grid field, dimension
    marks, leader lines, title-block stamps, registration ticks, half-inked
    glyphs, mono spec tables) is THE identity — new sections must speak it, not
    generic dark-SaaS (no glassmorphism, no glow orbs, no particle fields —
    that was Route B, rejected).

# Sprint 01 — Creative Direction — HANDOVER
status: PASS
commit: (filled at commit time — single sprint-01 commit on feat/rebuild-2026)
wall_clock: ~15

## What was built
- `plans/design/styleframes/route-{A,B,C}.html` — 3 standalone, self-contained
  candidate styleframes (Home vertical slice: nav + hero + CustomsIQ act +
  industries strip), openable via `file://`, CDN fonts throwaway-only.
- `plans/design/styleframes/route-{A,B,C}.png` + `route-{A,B,C}-full.png` —
  1440×900 viewport + full-page screenshots (Playwright Chromium, headless).
- `plans/design/candidates/route-A-blueprint.md`, `route-B-genesis.md`,
  `route-C-kinetic-index.md` — per-candidate specs (mood/palette/type/motion
  thesis/WebGL moments/IA/reference anchors).
- `plans/design/JUDGE.md` — 3-critic panel (Awwwards juror, brand-fit,
  perf/feasibility), full per-axis score tables, weighted composites, verdict.
- `plans/design/DIRECTION.md` — THE binding art direction (headers: Tokens /
  IA / Motion Language / Showpieces / Anti-Wander Rules; carries the 14 motion
  laws + all 10 scars + tier matrix).
- `plans/design/TOKENS.md` — machine token source (palette table + CSS vars,
  type families + WOFF2 plan, type scale clamps, spacing/radii, easing
  constants, Tailwind wiring sketch).
- `plans/CONTRACTS.md` — S01 sections appended: token pointer (var prefix
  `--k-`, Tailwind keys, easing constants), locked showpiece selector names,
  skyline mount-point row + pipeline-placement supersession note.

## Decisions made
- **WINNER: Route A — "Blueprint → Built"** (unanimous 3/3; composite A 4.72,
  C 4.25, B 2.73). Rationale: only route where CustomsIQ/HS-codes/₹ figures
  feel native; every act buildable from SVG/line/type (no particle sims); the
  reduced-motion still IS the design.
- **Accent committed: blueprint blue `#3B7CFF`** (not acid lime) — semantically
  "live blueprint line", distinct from old indigo.
- **Type committed:** Clash Display 600/700 + Satoshi (var) + JetBrains Mono
  (var); 4 self-hosted WOFF2 ≤ 220 KB; NO serif accent (that was Route C).
- **H1 hero showpiece DOWNGRADED to CSS/SVG** (brief explicitly allows) — only
  H2 (CustomsIQ) + W2 (skyline) ship WebGL. Keeps Home initial bundle lean.
- **W2 technique: instanced-extrude** (single instanced draw, 9 volumes), not
  the image-reveal shader (that was the C path).
- **Easing committed:** `EASE.PRIMARY = cubic-bezier(0.7, 0, 0.2, 1)`
  (CustomEase "kPrimary"), `EASE.SETTLE = "expo.out"`, `EASE.SNAP =
  "back.out(1.4)"` (grid-lock only); durations 0.3/0.5/0.6/0.7s, scene 1.0s.
- **Grafts from runner-up C:** velocity-skew on the industries band (the one
  allowed skew) + index/table-of-contents rhythm (numbered rows, hairline rules).
- Screenshot tooling: repo's `playwright-core` + cached Chromium (no new deps).

## Deviations from the prompt / CONTRACTS.md
- CONTRACTS.md skeleton had `data-showpiece="pipeline"` on `/work`; the binding
  brief storyboard (LOCKED) puts CustomsIQ on **Home H2** and the 9-industry
  skyline on **`/work` W2**. Resolution per append rules: struck the route cell
  (not deleted), added the `skyline` row, left a note. **S02 must rewrite the
  Route map to match DIRECTION.md §IA; orchestrator should re-point S06/S07
  showpiece ownership accordingly** (pipeline mount = Home page sprint S06;
  skyline mount = Work page sprint S07).
- PROTOCOL §3's app-QA steps (build/verify/facts_check/walkthrough) not run —
  this sprint touches zero production code by design; the sprint file §4/§6
  prescribes a docs/artifact gate instead (all checks passed, see QA result).
- No inherited dirty tree (started clean).

## Current visual state
- The shipped app is UNCHANGED (old site still builds/renders as before).
- Styleframes (not shipped, audit artifacts): route-A = graphite drafting table
  w/ blueprint-blue schematic + agent.trace; route-B = deep-space particle
  constellation; route-C = light editorial bone/oxblood giant type.
- screenshot dir: `plans/design/styleframes/` (committed; this docs sprint's
  equivalent of /tmp/shots/sprint-01/).

## Contracts touched
- CONTRACTS.md → Design tokens pointer finalized: token files
  `plans/design/TOKENS.md` + `DIRECTION.md`; **CSS var prefix `--k-`**;
  Tailwind keys `ground/surface/surface-2/line/line-2/ink/ink-dim/muted/
  muted-2/accent/accent-strong`; fonts + easing constants.
- CONTRACTS.md → Mount-point table: added `data-showpiece="skyline"` (`/work`);
  noted pipeline lives on `/` per DIRECTION; selector NAMES locked:
  `hero` (CSS/SVG slot), `pipeline`, `skyline`.
- New tokens: see TOKENS.md §1–§5 (S02 implements verbatim).

## Instructions + WARNINGS for next sprint
- **S02 (scaffold):** implement TOKENS.md §1–§4 + §6 verbatim
  (tailwind.config + `:root` `--k-*` vars); 4 routes
  `/ /work /studio /contact` with `react-router-dom` (pre-approved) +
  GitHub Pages SPA fallback (`public/404.html` sessionStorage trick + restore
  snippet in `index.html`, CNAME at root AND `public/CNAME`); create
  `src/data/*` from `plans/00-facts/facts.json`; self-host the 4 WOFF2 fonts
  (Fontshare: Clash Display 600/700 + Satoshi var; Google: JetBrains Mono var)
  in `public/fonts/`, Latin subset + `₹ → · ✓` glyphs, kill the Google-CDN
  font link; REWRITE the CONTRACTS Route map per DIRECTION.md §IA.
- **Key facts for the orchestrator:** (a) winner = Route A "Blueprint→Built",
  palette graphite `#0C0D10` / bone `#EDE7DA` / accent `#3B7CFF`, type Clash
  Display + Satoshi + JetBrains Mono; (b) LOCKED IA = `/` `/work` `/studio`
  `/contact` with act orders in DIRECTION.md §IA — pipeline showpiece is on
  HOME, skyline on WORK (re-point S06–S09/S10–S12 if their prompts assume
  otherwise); (c) 3 showpiece briefs in DIRECTION.md §Showpieces — H2+W2 WebGL,
  **H1 is CSS/SVG (no third canvas — S10's scope shrinks to the GL foundation
  + pipeline prep, not a hero canvas)**; (d) token values for S02 in TOKENS.md.
- Per-route initial JS budget note: brief §4 says Home ≤ 180 KB (tighter than
  PROTOCOL's 220 KB) — treat 180 as the design target, 220 as the hard gate.
- Body copy contrast rule: never `accent` (#3B7CFF) or `muted-2` for body text
  (axe color-contrast gate). `ink`/`ink-dim` on `ground`/`surface` are safe.
- The styleframe HTMLs are throwaway references — do NOT import or ship them.
- Do NOT touch `main`; do not push; `feat/rebuild-2026` only.

## QA result
- verify.mjs: not run (no production code — docs sprint per sprint file §4/§6)
- facts_check.mjs: not run (facts.json untouched; src untouched)
- build: not run (no app code changed; tree clean of src/public/config)
- sprint §6 artifact gate: 6/6 PASS (headers, tokens, candidates+judge+frames,
  winner named, forbidden-color grep clean, zero production files touched)
- console errors: n/a (styleframes screenshotted with zero pageerrors)

## Visual audit
- auditor verdict: SHIP (the 3-critic judge panel WAS the fresh-eyes audit —
  critics received only screenshots + spec docs + rubric, no build narrative;
  winning frame scored 4.65–4.75/5 weighted across all three critics)
- findings: route-A hero polyhedron "reads slightly inert" in the still
  (Awwwards critic) → accepted: it is the static fallback frame by design; the
  built H1 gets the 6s SVG breathe loop (DIRECTION.md §Showpieces brief 3).
  Distinctiveness risk "dark ground + blue accent adjacent to dark-SaaS" →
  mitigated via Anti-Wander rule 10 (drafting-table vocabulary mandatory,
  glassmorphism/glow/particles banned).

The kartar.ai full-UI rebuild (2026-06-11) runs as an unattended **sprint chain** on branch `feat/rebuild-2026` (cut from main; main/live site never touched, nothing pushed). Supersedes the one-shot approach of [[first-light-overhaul]] (that branch's design is scratch; its QA harness was ported).

Launch kit (committed on the branch):
- `plans/PROTOCOL.md` — binding rules incl. the 10 scars (scars #1–#8 from first-light + #9 router: kill route triggers before next page mounts + #10 GL: dispose canvases on route-leave). `plans/CONTRACTS.md` is the living contract sprints append to.
- `plans/00-facts/facts.json` + `FACTS.md` — the data contract (18 team LinkedIn URLs, 9 industry stats, 5 pipeline stages, EmailJS/Sender/Calendly IDs). Copy is free-rewrite; facts are asserted by `scripts/qa/facts_check.mjs`.
- `plans/design/DIRECTION_BRIEF.md` — pre-pinned creative brief: 3 candidates (A Blueprint→Built / B Genesis / C Kinetic Index), judge rubric, LOCKED 4-route IA (/ /work /studio /contact). Sprint 01 writes DIRECTION.md + TOKENS.md.
- `plans/sprints/sprint-01..17-*.md` — the chain (16 = adversarial UX-audit panel, 17 = report). Pipeline showpiece (CustomsIQ assemble/disassemble) is on HOME (`data-showpiece="pipeline"`, act H2); Work gets `data-showpiece="skyline"`.
- QA gate has 8 conditions incl. a mandatory **fresh-eyes visual audit subagent** (reads every screenshot, SHIP|FIX verdict) and an **axe-core WCAG AA gate** in verify.mjs (serious/critical incl. color-contrast fail non-`legacy` routes; the pre-rebuild "/" route carries `legacy: true` in routes.mjs = informational only).
- `plans/run-sprints.sh` — orchestrator. Status markers are FILES: `plans/status/sprint-NN.PASS|FAIL|SKIPPED`. Double gate = exit 0 AND .PASS. Retry once → MAX_FIXERS fixers → SKIPPED, chain continues. Resume = rerun (PASS/SKIPPED skipped). Graceful stop: `touch plans/STOP`. Smoke-test with `SPRINTS_DIR=/tmp/... SKIP_PREFLIGHT=1`.
- Launch: `caffeinate -dimsu nohup bash plans/run-sprints.sh > plans/logs/launch.out 2>&1 &`; observer prompt in `plans/MONITOR_PROMPT.md`.

**Why:** two one-shot overhauls (REVAMP_PROMPT v1/v2) built fine but were creatively rejected and never merged; per-sprint QA gates + handovers + a pre-pinned direction contest fix that failure mode.

**How to apply:** any future sprint/QA edit must keep `scripts/qa/routes.mjs` in sync with the router (S02+), keep marker-file naming exact (no `.status` scheme), and never rename `data-showpiece` selectors without updating CONTRACTS.md. Model string for headless chains: `claude-fable-5` (NOT claude-opus-4-8 — that's the fallback). CLI was updated to 2.1.173 on 2026-06-11 (Fable 5 needs ≥2.1.170). GNU `timeout` is absent on this Mac; `sort -V` works. `dist/` is untracked on this branch (CI builds it). See [[gsap-scrolltrigger-kill-in-onupdate]].

The kartar.ai full-UI rebuild (2026-06-11) runs as an unattended **sprint chain** on branch `feat/rebuild-2026` (cut from main; main/live site never touched, nothing pushed). Supersedes the one-shot approach of [[first-light-overhaul]] (that branch's design is scratch; its QA harness was ported).

Launch kit (committed on the branch):
- `plans/PROTOCOL.md` — binding rules incl. the 10 scars (scars #1–#8 from first-light + #9 router: kill route triggers before next page mounts + #10 GL: dispose canvases on route-leave). `plans/CONTRACTS.md` is the living contract sprints append to.
- `plans/00-facts/facts.json` + `FACTS.md` — the data contract (18 team LinkedIn URLs, 9 industry stats, 5 pipeline stages, EmailJS/Sender/Calendly IDs). Copy is free-rewrite; facts are asserted by `scripts/qa/facts_check.mjs`.
- `plans/design/DIRECTION_BRIEF.md` — pre-pinned creative brief: 3 candidates (A Blueprint→Built / B Genesis / C Kinetic Index), judge rubric, LOCKED 4-route IA (/ /work /studio /contact). Sprint 01 writes DIRECTION.md + TOKENS.md.
- `plans/sprints/sprint-01..17-*.md` — the chain (16 = adversarial UX-audit panel, 17 = report). Pipeline showpiece (CustomsIQ assemble/disassemble) is on HOME (`data-showpiece="pipeline"`, act H2); Work gets `data-showpiece="skyline"`.
- QA gate has 8 conditions incl. a mandatory **fresh-eyes visual audit subagent** (reads every screenshot, SHIP|FIX verdict) and an **axe-core WCAG AA gate** in verify.mjs (serious/critical incl. color-contrast fail non-`legacy` routes; the pre-rebuild "/" route carries `legacy: true` in routes.mjs = informational only).
- `plans/run-sprints.sh` — orchestrator. Status markers are FILES: `plans/status/sprint-NN.PASS|FAIL|SKIPPED`. Double gate = exit 0 AND .PASS. Retry once → MAX_FIXERS fixers → SKIPPED, chain continues. Resume = rerun (PASS/SKIPPED skipped). Graceful stop: `touch plans/STOP`. Smoke-test with `SPRINTS_DIR=/tmp/... SKIP_PREFLIGHT=1`.
- Launch: `caffeinate -dimsu nohup bash plans/run-sprints.sh > plans/logs/launch.out 2>&1 &`; observer prompt in `plans/MONITOR_PROMPT.md`.

**Why:** two one-shot overhauls (REVAMP_PROMPT v1/v2) built fine but were creatively rejected and never merged; per-sprint QA gates + handovers + a pre-pinned direction contest fix that failure mode.

**How to apply:** any future sprint/QA edit must keep `scripts/qa/routes.mjs` in sync with the router (S02+), keep marker-file naming exact (no `.status` scheme), and never rename `data-showpiece` selectors without updating CONTRACTS.md. Model string for headless chains: `claude-fable-5` (NOT claude-opus-4-8 — that's the fallback). CLI was updated to 2.1.173 on 2026-06-11 (Fable 5 needs ≥2.1.170). GNU `timeout` is absent on this Mac; `sort -V` works. `dist/` is untracked on this branch (CI builds it). See [[gsap-scrolltrigger-kill-in-onupdate]].

**Sprint 01 — PASS.** Commit `ed8abc02` on `feat/rebuild-2026`, tree clean, marker + handover written.

```diff
+ [DECISION] Winner = Route A "Blueprint → Built" — teeno critics (Awwwards / brand-fit / perf) ne unanimously A chuna: composite 4.72 vs C 4.25 vs B 2.73.
+ [KEY] Tokens lock ho gaye: graphite #0C0D10 ground, bone #EDE7DA ink, ek hi accent #3B7CFF blueprint-blue (≤10%), Clash Display + Satoshi + JetBrains Mono.
+ [DECISION] H1 hero showpiece WebGL se CSS/SVG pe downgrade — ab sirf 2 GL showpieces shippenge (H2 CustomsIQ on Home, W2 skyline on /work). Home bundle lean rahega.
+ [GOTCHA] CONTRACTS.md ke skeleton mein pipeline /work pe tha, par brief ke hisaab se CustomsIQ HOME (H2) pe hai — supersession note daala; S02 ko Route map rewrite karna hoga.
```

**What happened:**
- **Built**: 3 standalone styleframes (`plans/design/styleframes/route-{A,B,C}.html` + 6 PNGs at 1440×900), 3 candidate specs, all using real facts (All Is. Now, Read→Ground→Reason→Classify→Cite, HS 8471.30, ₹2,41,800).
- **Judged**: 3 fresh-eyes critic agents scored only the pixels + specs against the brief's weighted rubric. A won on fit (5.0), achievability (5.0 — pure SVG/line/type, no particle sims), and mobile degrade (5.0 — the static still *is* the design). B (Genesis) rejected as a generic, mystical, fragile particle cliché; C's best ideas (velocity-skew band, index rhythm) grafted into A.
- **Bound**: `DIRECTION.md` (all 5 required headers, 14 motion laws + 10 scars restated), `TOKENS.md` (palette/type-scale/easings, `--k-` vars), `JUDGE.md` (full score tables + winner A), CONTRACTS.md seeded with locked selector names (`hero`/`pipeline`/`skyline`).
- **Gate**: all 6 §6 checks passed (headers, token greps, artifacts, winner named, forbidden-color grep clean, zero production files touched). `plans/status/sprint-01.PASS` + complete handover written; one commit, nothing pushed, `main` untouched. Spend: ~$11 of the $25 budget.
